Title: Data analyst
Description:
Join our mission! As our new Data Analyst




Yuki stands at the forefront of the accounting and financial administration revolution. Rapidly expanding and driven by our innovative software solutions, we proudly serve more than 70,000 entrepreneurs and hundreds of accountancy firms across Belgium.




At Yuki, we believe better data leads to better decisions, better products, and better customer experiences. To help us strengthen that foundation, we are looking for a Data Analyst to join our Data & RevOps team and help turn data into actionable insights across the business.




Your mission at Yuki




As a Data Analyst within the Data & RevOps team, you help Yuki become a more data-informed organization by partnering closely with stakeholders across Product, Marketing, Sales, Customer Success, and other business teams.




Your goal is to translate business questions into clear metrics, trusted reporting, and actionable insights. You combine analytical depth with strong stakeholder management, helping teams make better decisions while ensuring data initiatives stay aligned with business priorities.




What You Get To Do




Drive data-backed decisions: Partner with stakeholders across Product, Marketing, Sales, and Customer Success to identify key business questions and deliver actionable insights that support strategic and operational decision-making.
Analyze product and customer behavior: Work on user, funnel, and lifecycle analytics to better understand activation, retention, churn, engagement, and expansion patterns.
Build and improve reporting: Create and refine dashboards and reports that are reliable, user-friendly, and help the business make informed decisions.
Define and align KPIs: Work with stakeholders to define, document, and maintain consistent KPI logic and reporting standards across teams.
Work with data sources and reporting layers: Combine and analyze data from different domains, including Product, Sales, Marketing, Customer Success, and Finance, to create a stronger single source of truth.
Support the evolution of the data landscape: Help document data structures, reporting logic, and key processes so the team can work more efficiently in an evolving data environment.
Improve ways of working: Take a proactive role in improving reporting approaches, stakeholder processes, and analytics practices to keep Yuki’s data setup scalable and effective.

What We’re Looking For




We’re looking for someone who enjoys diving into data, translating complexity into clarity, and working closely with stakeholders to solve meaningful business problems.




At least 3 years of relevant experience in Data Analytics, Business Analytics, Product Analytics, or a similar role
Strong experience with SQL and working with data warehouses or structured reporting environments
Experience building dashboards and reports in BI tools such as Power BI, Looker, Tableau, or similar
Strong analytical thinking and the ability to turn data into clear, actionable insights
Experience working with stakeholders across multiple business teams and managing reporting or analytics requests effectively
Ability to understand, challenge, and prioritize business needs in a practical way
Familiarity with data integration, reporting layers, or ETL / ELT concepts is a plus
Comfortable working in a fast-paced environment where priorities can evolve
Strong communication skills in English, both written and spoken
Dutch is a plus

Most importantly, you are curious, proactive, and motivated to help teams make better decisions through data.




What’s In It For You




Personal development: A generous training budget available from day one for certifications, courses, training, and more to strengthen your expertise
Time off and flexibility: A hybrid working model and generous time-off policies, because we believe work-life balance is important
Well-being & focus: Access to lifestyle coaches, lunches, and other initiatives that contribute to your well-being and focus
Financial security: A competitive salary package with attractive benefits, such as Flex Income Plan (FIP), hospitalization insurance, and a pension plan
Optimal workplace: The best hardware, including an Apple or Dell laptop
Plenty of opportunities: Enjoy the speed and autonomy of an innovative scale-up, backed by the stability, vast resources, and global career network of the Visma Group
Team & international culture: Participate in team-building days and Yuki events that strengthen the close team spirit in a diverse and international environment

What The Process Will Look Like




Apply Apply now, and we’ll get back to you as soon as possible.
Recruiter screen A first video call with our Talent Acquisition team.
First interview An initial interview with the hiring team to explore your experience, motivation, and fit for the role.
Case study A take-home case to showcase how you approach real Revenue Operations challenges.
Technical & presentation interview Present your case and discuss your functional and technical approach with the hiring team.
Culture fit interview Meet additional stakeholders to assess collaboration style and overall fit.
Offer If we see a match, you’ll receive an offer.

Title: Data-analist
Description:
Ter ondersteuning van onze verdere digitalisering en procesoptimalisatie zijn we op zoek naar een Master Data & Power BI Specialist.

Binnen onze organisatie vervul je een sleutelrol in het beheren, bewaken en optimaliseren van masterdata, PIM en rapportering. Je zorgt ervoor dat data betrouwbaar, consistent en bruikbaar is als fundament voor operationele en strategische beslissingen.




Hoe ziet jouw takenpakket eruit?




Je staat in voor de governance en beheer van masterdata in onze ERP-omgeving (artikelen, leveranciers, klanten).
Je bent verantwoordelijk voor het beheer, de opvolging en verdere optimalisatie van onze PIM-omgeving (Pimcore), waarbij je waakt over datakwaliteit, volledigheid en consistentie.
Je werkt samen met verschillende stakeholders om productinformatie correct te structureren en beschikbaar te maken voor e-commerce, marketing en externe platformen.
Je ontwerpt, beheert en optimaliseert Power BI-dashboards die inzicht geven in verkoop, supply chain en operationele prestaties.
Je analyseert bestaande datastromen en processen en stelt proactief verbeteringen voor om efficiëntie, transparantie en databetrouwbaarheid te verhogen.
Je werkt nauw samen met verschillende afdelingen (Supply Chain, Finance, Sales, Marketing, IT, …) om nieuwe producten, processen en wijzigingen correct te integreren in de systemen.
Je fungeert als intern aanspreekpunt voor masterdata en rapportering en ondersteunt collega’s bij het interpreteren van cijfers en dashboards.
Naast je vaste verantwoordelijkheden neem je ook deel aan en volg je extra projecten op die gericht zijn op procesverbetering, digitalisering en het verhogen van de operationele efficiëntie binnen Allinox. Daarbij is het belangrijk dat je openstaat voor nieuwe ontwikkelingen op de markt, waaronder AI-toepassingen in diverse domeinen.




Profiel – wat verwachten we van jou?




Je beschikt over een bachelor- of masterdiploma of bent gelijkwaardig door ervaring in data management, IT of een analytische administratieve rol.
Je werkt vlot met Excel, hebt ervaring met ERP-systemen en beschikt over goede basiskennis van Power BI, ervaring met SQL is een plus
Je hebt een analytische en nauwkeurige mindset en werkt gestructureerd met complexe datasets.
Je communiceert vlot in het Nederlands en Engels en werkt graag samen met verschillende teams.




Aanbod

• Een voltijdse functie in een warm en ambitieus familiebedrijf met ruimte voor persoonlijke

ontwikkeling.

• Een gevarieerd takenpakket met verantwoordelijkheid.

• Je komt terecht in een jong en dynamisch team met een open mentaliteit en een aangename werksfeer.

• 20 wettelijke verlofdagen plus 15 extra ADV-dagen bij een fulltime contract.

• Een aantrekkelijk verloningspakket.




Allinox BV is een snelgroeiend en succesvol familiebedrijf, gespecialiseerd in ontwikkeling, productie en verkoop van kwaliteitsvolle kookmaterialen,... met kantoren in België, Frankrijk,

de Verenigde Staten en Hong Kong. Vanuit het hoofdkantoor in Oostrozebeke (regio Waregem)

worden de eigen, in-house ontwikkelde merken Beka (www.beka-cookware.com) en Alva

(www.alva-cookware.com) wereldwijd uitgerold, evenals de Brabantia cookware licentie. Daarnaast

specialiseert Allinox zich in het aanbieden van een uiterst gevarieerd assortiment van private label

programma’s voor verschillende klanten wereldwijd, op maat van iedere klant.

Description:
Nexeo is currently looking for a Data Analyst (Freelance or permanent) for a market leader in the banking industry in Belgium.




Nexeo Belgium is a consultancy company that supports its clients in the banking, finance and insurance sectors in all phases of their transformation projects: from business consulting to the implementation of IT solutions. 




As a consultant you will have the opportunity to be part of Nexeo:

International environment ( Offices in France, Belgium, Luxembourg, Switzerland & Canada)
+ 450 employees
over 20% growth each year




The ideal candidate will use their passion for big data and analytics to provide insights to the business covering a range of topics. They will be responsible for conducting both recurring and ad hoc analysis for business users.

Understand the day-to-day issues that the bank faces
Performs cleansing of data quality issues through the analysi

Business Analysis
Work in collaboration with other technical teams to share data
Data extraction with SAS
Industrialize the process to run define periodicity

Contribute to the achievement of the team objectives

Understand data lifecycle and data usage
Working in Agile environnement (Jira & Scrum)
Good experience working with SQL Queries & SAS
English Fluent & French or Dutch native

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
At darwind, we are looking for a Data Consultant who enjoys bringing clarity to complexity and turning raw information into meaningful insights. You help organisations build strong data foundations, enabling better decision‑making and long‑term value creation. You will work in an environment that values autonomy, responsibility and professional growth.




What you will do

In this role, you support end‑to‑end data initiatives across business and technical teams. You will:

Collect, clean and transform data from various sources.
Build dashboards and visualisations using Power BI, Tableau or Excel.
Write and optimise SQL queries for reporting and analysis.
Support or develop data pipelines (ETL/ELT) and contribute to cloud‑based data environments.
Model datasets and design efficient, scalable data structures.
Identify patterns, trends and insights that support business decisions.
Collaborate with analysts, IT teams and business stakeholders.
Improve data quality, documentation and data governance practices.
Present findings clearly to both technical and non‑technical audiences.




Who you are

You bring structure, analytical thinking and clear communication. You also have:

A Master’s degree.
At least three years of experience in data management or a similar field.
Strong knowledge of SQL and at least one analytics or visualisation tool (Power BI, Tableau or Excel).
Understanding of data modelling, data structures and relational databases.
The ability to translate complex data into clear business insights.
A proactive, accurate and curious mindset.

HTML:
At darwind, we are looking for a Data Consultant who enjoys bringing clarity to complexity and turning raw information into meaningful insights. You help organisations build strong data foundations, enabling better decision‑making and long‑term value creation. You will work in an environment that values autonomy, responsibility and professional growth.


What you will do

In this role, you support end‑to‑end data initiatives across business and technical teams. You will:

  • Collect, clean and transform data from various sources.
  • Build dashboards and visualisations using Power BI, Tableau or Excel.
  • Write and optimise SQL queries for reporting and analysis.
  • Support or develop data pipelines (ETL/ELT) and contribute to cloud‑based data environments.
  • Model datasets and design efficient, scalable data structures.
  • Identify patterns, trends and insights that support business decisions.
  • Collaborate with analysts, IT teams and business stakeholders.
  • Improve data quality, documentation and data governance practices.
  • Present findings clearly to both technical and non‑technical audiences.


Who you are

You bring structure, analytical thinking and clear communication. You also have:

  • A Master’s degree.
  • At least three years of experience in data management or a similar field.
  • Strong knowledge of SQL and at least one analytics or visualisation tool (Power BI, Tableau or Excel).
  • Understanding of data modelling, data structures and relational databases.
  • The ability to translate complex data into clear business insights.
  • A proactive, accurate and curious mindset.
  • Experience in cloud environments is considered an advantage.
  • Fluency in Dutch, with proficiency in French as a plus.


What we offer

We provide an environment where impact, ownership and development are central.

Competitive benefits package

  • Hospitalization insurance
  • Group insurance
  • Meal allowance
  • Your choice between a company car with EU fuel card or the federal mobility budget


Flexibility and work‑life balance

  • Flexible working hours
  • Projects aligned with your skills and ambitions


Learning and growth

  • Continuous coaching
  • Technical and soft‑skills training
  • Opportunities to grow across different domains and technologies


Culture

  • A human, transparent and collaborative environment
  • Real ownership and influence on the projects you contribute to

If you want to work on projects where people, impact and growth truly matter, we would be happy to meet you.

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Wil jij met data échte impact maken op het onderwijs van morgen?

Als Data Analist help je onze organisatie betere beslissingen nemen door data om te zetten in heldere inzichten. Je werkt met een moderne data stack (SQL, Power BI,…) en krijgt veel autonomie om end-to-end oplossingen uit te bouwen die ook écht gebruikt worden.

Je komt terecht in een mensgerichte organisatie waar data steeds belangrijker wordt en waar jouw werk zichtbaar bijdraagt aan maatschappelijke impact.

Jouw team

Je wordt onderdeel van het Data & Analytics team, waar je samenwerkt met één andere data-analist in België. Daarnaast heb je regelmatig contact met een groter team van data-analisten en experts in Nederland.

Via kennisdeling en best practices versterk je je expertise en draag je mee bij aan de verdere uitbouw van een data gedreven organisatie. Af en toe werk je ook samen op locatie in Den Bosch.

Wat ga je doen?

Als Data Analist werk je van businessvraag tot bruikbaar inzicht. Je fungeert als brug tussen data en besluitvorming en helpt collega’s data beter te begrijpen en te gebruiken.

Concreet Betekent Dit Dat Je


Businessvragen scherp stelt en vertaalt naar analyses en datavereisten
Data verzamelt, transformeert en modelleert uit verschillende bronnen
End-to-end oplossingen bouwt met SQL en Snowflake, Power BI en DBT is een mooie plus!
Heldere, actiegerichte dashboards en rapporten ontwikkelt die effectief gebruikt worden
Datakwaliteit, governance en betrouwbaarheid mee bewaakt
Inzichten en aanbevelingen duidelijk communiceert naar zowel technische als niet-technische stakeholders
Business teams ondersteunt in projecten en besluitvorming
Meebouwt aan de verdere professionalisering van processen, data infrastructuur en het dataplatform
Data literacy binnen de organisatie versterkt via begeleiding, workshops of kennisdeling


Wie zoeken wij?

Je hoeft niet alles al perfect te beheersen, leergierigheid en analytisch denkvermogen zijn minstens zo belangrijk.


Je hebt een bachelor diploma of gelijkwaardige ervaring
Je hebt 2 tot 4 jaar ervaring in data-analyse, BI of insights
Je werkt vlot met SQL en BI-tools
Ervaring met dbt, Power BI en Snowflake is een sterke troef, of je wil je hierin snel verdiepen
Kennis van Python is een plus
Je kan complexe informatie helder uitleggen aan verschillende doelgroepen
Je werkt gestructureerd, denkt analytisch en neemt ownership
Je bent een teamspeler die kennis deelt en graag bijleert
Je volgt trends in data, AI en analytics en denkt mee over toepassingen
Je kan professioneel communiceren in het Frans of bent bereid dit verder te versterken


Wat mag je van ons verwachten?


Een mensgerichte cultuur met een DNA waarbij veel aandacht gaat naar het welzijn en werkgeluk
Een betekenisvolle job met maatschappelijke impact
Werken binnen een sterke, internationale groep (SANOMA)
Ruimte om te groeien, leren en initiatief te nemen
Samenwerking met ervaren data-professionals in België en Nederland
Een competitief loon aangevuld met extralegale voordelen
Hybride werken: meerdere dagen per week thuiswerk mogelijk
Moderne kantoren in Wommelgem en Mont-Saint-Guibert, volledig uitgerust voor hybride samenwerking. Kijk alvast eens binnen in onze kantoren!


About Sanoma Learning

How do you prepare students for the world, engage their curiosity and inspire them to grow? That is the main question of teachers all around the world. At Sanoma Learning, it has always been our mission to help to find and develop best-in-class answers to this question. If you join us, it means that you embark on an exciting journey to help shape the future of learning for primary, secondary and vocational education. We believe that your journey is our success as a company.

Sanoma Learning is a leading K12 European learning company, operating in 12 countries. Our learning products and services enable and support teachers and schools to develop the talents of every child to reach their potential. By offering printed and digital learning content, as well as digital learning and teaching platforms for primary, secondary and vocational education, we want to grow our positive impact on learning across Europe. With over 3000 employees, we help shape the future of education for 25+ million of students.

So, are you ready to start your journey to help shape the future of learning and help teachers to support all students to reach their potential?

Sanoma Learning is an equal opportunity employer; we are committed to inclusion and diversity, ensure equal opportunity for all applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or any other characteristics.

Title: Job | Data Analyst - Pricing & Operational Performance | Bruxelles
Description:
Description

En tant que Technical Support Non-Vie, vous ferez partie intégrante du département Performance Management & Pricing, en vous concentrant sur la validation des données, l'industrialisation des modèles et le développement de tableaux de bord, permettant à Belfius de s’appuyer sur des analyses fiables et solides.

Vous évoluerez dans un environnement en pleine transformation, combinant data, cloud et nouvelles technologies.

Votre job en deux mots

En tant que Technical Support Non-Vie, vous rejoignez le département Performance Management & Pricing, où vous jouez un rôle clé à l’intersection entre data, actuariat et technologie.

Vous contribuez activement à la fiabilisation des données, à l’industrialisation des modèles et à la modernisation de nos outils, notamment dans le cadre de la migration vers le cloud.

Grâce à votre travail, Belfius s’appuie sur des analyses robustes, automatisées et scalables pour piloter sa performance.

Vous serez au cœur de plusieurs axes stratégiques :


Data & Validation
Mettre en place et maintenir une couche de validation des données actuarielles.
Garantir la qualité, la cohérence et la fiabilité des données.
Faciliter le travail des équipes Pricing (Business & Individuals).
Modernisation & Migration
Participer activement à la migration des solutions SAS vers des technologies cloud (PySpark, Azure, …).
Développer des solutions modernes en utilisant des outils tels que VS Code, Git et Copilot.
Industrialiser les pipelines de données et améliorer leur performance.
Analyse & Reporting
Développer des Dashboards (BI) et des rapports (QoS, QBR, etc.).
Réaliser des analyses opérationnelles (Customer satisfaction, STP, SLA…).
Identifier des leviers d’amélioration de la performance et de la rentabilité.
Engineering & Best Practices
Produire du code structuré, documenté et maintenable (documentation via MkDocs, docstrings, guidelines d’équipe…)
Contribuer à la mise en place de standards de développement et bonnes pratiques
Tester, valider et améliorer en continu les processus existants

Pas besoin d’être un oiseau rare

Chez nous, vous pouvez vous distinguer et révéler le meilleur de vous-même si vous répondez aux critères suivants :


Vous avez un diplôme STIM (Sciences, Technologies, Ingénierie, Mathématique, Informatique) ou sciences économiques/commerciales et/ou une expérience utile.
Vous avez une expérience professionnelle de 2 à 3 ans dans un domaine pertinent.
Vous avez un réel intérêt pour les nouvelles technologies et une curiosité naturelle pour les innovations et les nouvelles approches
Vous avez de bonnes compétences en programmation (Python, SQL, …) et êtes à l’aise avec l’utilisation de packages.
Vous connaissez les bases des outils de versioning, notamment Git.
Vous vous intéressez au cloud et aux architectures modernes
Vous possédez de bonnes compétences en analyse de données, modélisation et bases de données
Vous communiquez facilement et appréciez le travail en équipe
Vous faites preuve d'un esprit critique et analytique.
Vous adoptez une approche précise, pragmatique et efficace.
Vous avez une personnalité dynamique et proactive avec une passion pour les chiffres et les données.



Vous marquerez encore plus de points si :


Vous êtes bilingue (anglais-français ou néerlandais).
Vous avez des connaissances en PySpark et SAS .
Vous avez déjà travaillé avec des technologies cloud (Azure, Synapse, ML, …) ou des outils de data visualisation (QlikSense).
Vous avez une expérience avec des outils de collaboration et de développement comme Azure DevOps.
Vous accordez de l’importance aux détails et à la qualité.
Vous êtes capable de résumer une foule de données et de les traduire en visions clés.



Belfius prend soin de ses talents

Outre la possibilité de donner le meilleur de vous-même dans un job très intéressant, nous vous offrons un contrat à temps plein à durée indéterminée avec :


Un salaire attractif complété par une offre innovante vous octroyant un budget à utiliser selon vos préférences personnelles (p. ex. voiture, vélo, PC, smartphone, tablette, loyer ou crédit hypothécaire sous certaines conditions) et des avantages extralégaux, tels que des chèques-repas, des avantages sur les produits et services internes, une assurance hospitalisation et une assurance groupe.
Des possibilités de développement par le biais d'un large éventail de formations et des opportunités de carrière dans l’ensemble du groupe Belfius.
L'accès à une Actuarial Community sympathique, qui prend régulièrement des initiatives pour organiser des formations techniques ou un partage de l’expertise entre collègues.
Un bon équilibre entre vie professionnelle et vie privée grâce à des horaires flexibles, à la combinaison du travail au bureau et à domicile et un nombre attractif de jours de congé.
Un lieu de travail moderne au cœur de Bruxelles, avec une salle de sport branchée, un délicieux restaurant sain et des espaces de réunion confortables, le tout à quelques pas de la rue commerciale, de la gare Bruxelles-Nord et de la station métro Rogier.

Title: Analyst Data Operations
Description:
At Johnson & Johnson, we believe health is everything. Our strength in healthcare innovation empowers us to build a world where complex diseases are prevented, treated, and cured, where treatments are smarter and less invasive, and solutions are personal. Through our expertise in Innovative Medicine and MedTech, we are uniquely positioned to innovate across the full spectrum of healthcare solutions today to deliver the breakthroughs of tomorrow, and profoundly impact health for humanity. Learn more at jnj.com

As guided by Our Credo, Johnson & Johnson is responsible to our employees who work with us throughout the world. We provide an inclusive work environment where each person is considered as an individual. At Johnson & Johnson, we respect the diversity and dignity of our employees and recognize their merit.

Job Function

Data Analytics & Computational Sciences

Job Sub Function

Business Intelligence

Job Category

Professional

All Job Posting Locations:

Beerse, Antwerp, Belgium, Latina, Italy, Leiden, South Holland, Netherlands, Raritan, New Jersey, United States of America, Ringaskiddy, Cork, Ireland, Schaffhausen, Switzerland, Titusville, New Jersey, United States of America

Job Description

At Johnson & Johnson, we believe health is everything. Our strength in healthcare innovation empowers us to build a world where complex diseases are prevented, treated, and cured, where treatments are smarter and less invasive, and solutions are personal. Through our expertise in Innovative Medicine and MedTech, we are uniquely positioned to innovate across the full spectrum of healthcare solutions today to deliver the breakthroughs of tomorrow, and profoundly impact health for humanity. Learn more at https://www.jnj.com/

  

As we are building out our Data Strategy and Operations organization as part of our Value Chain Management organization in the Innovative Medicine Supply Chain we are recruiting for this role as part of our Master Data Operations team located in in either Titusville, NJ; Raritan, NJ; Zug, CH; Schaffhausen, CH; Leiden, NL; Cork, IE; Latina, IT or Beerse, BE. 

Please note that this role is available across multiple countries and may be posted under different requisition numbers to comply with local requirements. While you are welcome to apply to any or all of the postings, we recommend focusing on the specific country(s) that align with your preferred location(s):

United States - Req#: R-079226

EU - Req# R-082185

Remember, whether you apply to one or all of these requisition numbers, your applications will be considered as a single submission.

The IMSC MDM Operations team delivers trusted data to enable the launch & supply of products to our customers. Our key accountabilities include governance and maintenance of the Master Data Operations Framework; delivering a regulated and compliant data environment; using intelligent automation, workflow, process improvements, and third-party suppliers to improve quality, timeliness, cost, and effort across Supply Chain; applying deep end-to-end knowledge and subject matter expertise to define, shape and implement supply chain strategies; and cultivating talent as a distinctive capability. 

This position will execute day-to-day master data management operations. Will develop solutions to a variety of problems of moderate scope and complexity, while referring to established policies and procedures. 

Major Duties & Responsibilities: 

Responsible for the timely, efficient and accurate coordination, creation and maintenance of various master data elements (e.g. Material Master, BOM/Recipes, Production Versions,etc.) in SAP/ECC directly and/or through automated workflow system(s) and the execution of impact analyses or feasibility studies of change requests so that material planning, production, release, finance or other processes can proceed optimally.

Execute the data operations and data quality process. May include coordination of testing, error handling, data conversion and/or validation, and audit/compliance actions (Non-Conformance (NC) and/or Corrective Action Preventive Action (CAPA)).

Engage with partners on tactical matters. Provide tactical guidance and knowledge transfer to third-party team(s) and serve as point of escalation.

Participate in the completion of projects and departmental goals by tactically focusing on business outcomes. Execute tools and processes that improve master data management operations execution.


Collaborate on the preparation of metrics, dashboards, and tracking to facilitate the efficient operations of the department. 
Participate in, and sometimes lead, operational and governance meetings including end-to-end, value capture, and third-party reviews.  
Collaborate to create and maintain documentation for area of responsibility. May include Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), Work Instructions (WIs), protocols, data quality catalogs, and other policy and procedure documents. Deliver training to educate partners on detailed procedures, data flows, etc. 
Recommend creative solutions and participate in exploring refinement and implementation of those solutions. 
Support the deployment of the Data Management Framework 


Qualifications:  

Education


Minimum of a Bachelor’s or equivalent University Degree required


 

Required Knowledge, Skills and Abilities:  


A minimum of 2 years of relevant professional business experience is required.  
Strong experience, preferably within the health care or FMCG industry.
data processing systems (e.g. SAP required, OMP+ preferred)
procedures & Tools for processing and managing Master Data
GxP requirements
Basic Business and/or technical knowledge and understanding of Supply Chain data elements, process, and organizational support models.
MS Office Suite (Excel, Outlook, Word). 
Strong written, oral, and presentation skills. 
Strong organizational and prioritization skills. 
Ability to build strong collaborative relationships, influence others within job area through explanation of facts, policies and practices 
Ability to contribute on a diverse, multicultural and remote team. 
Ability to prioritize multiple initiatives within timelines, in a matrix environment where priorities change rapidly.  
English fluently required, local language skills strongly preferred
This position may require up to 5% of domestic and international (US) travel.  
Supervisor/people management experience is not required.


Key Working Relationships: 


Internal: IMSC MDM team, Value Chain Management roles eg. Supply Chain development leads, Planning functions such as E2E Planning, Site Planning, Finance, MAKE Operations, Quality assurance

Professional

Job Description

At Johnson & Johnson, we believe health is everything. Our strength in healthcare innovation empowers us to build a world where complex diseases are prevented, treated, and cured, where treatments are smarter and less invasive, and solutions are personal. Through our expertise in Innovative Medicine and MedTech, we are uniquely positioned to innovate across the full spectrum of healthcare solutions today to deliver the breakthroughs of tomorrow, and profoundly impact health for humanity. Learn more at https://www.jnj.com/

  

As we are building out our Data Strategy and Operations organization as part of our Value Chain Management organization in the Innovative Medicine Supply Chain we are recruiting for this role as part of our Master Data Operations team located in in either Titusville, NJ; Raritan, NJ; Zug, CH; Schaffhausen, CH; Leiden, NL; Cork, IE; Latina, IT or Beerse, BE. 

Please note that this role is available across multiple countries and may be posted under different requisition numbers to comply with local requirements. While you are welcome to apply to any or all of the postings, we recommend focusing on the specific country(s) that align with your preferred location(s):

United States - Req#: R-079226

EU - Req# R-082185

Remember, whether you apply to one or all of these requisition numbers, your applications will be considered as a single submission.

The IMSC MDM Operations team delivers trusted data to enable the launch & supply of products to our customers. Our key accountabilities include governance and maintenance of the Master Data Operations Framework; delivering a regulated and compliant data environment; using intelligent automation, workflow, process improvements, and third-party suppliers to improve quality, timeliness, cost, and effort across Supply Chain; applying deep end-to-end knowledge and subject matter expertise to define, shape and implement supply chain strategies; and cultivating talent as a distinctive capability. 

This position will execute day-to-day master data management operations. Will develop solutions to a variety of problems of moderate scope and complexity, while referring to established policies and procedures. 

Major Duties & Responsibilities: 

Responsible for the timely, efficient and accurate coordination, creation and maintenance of various master data elements (e.g. Material Master, BOM/Recipes, Production Versions,etc.) in SAP/ECC directly and/or through automated workflow system(s) and the execution of impact analyses or feasibility studies of change requests so that material planning, production, release, finance or other processes can proceed optimally.

Execute the data operations and data quality process. May include coordination of testing, error handling, data conversion and/or validation, and audit/compliance actions (Non-Conformance (NC) and/or Corrective Action Preventive Action (CAPA)).

Engage with partners on tactical matters. Provide tactical guidance and knowledge transfer to third-party team(s) and serve as point of escalation.

Participate in the completion of projects and departmental goals by tactically focusing on business outcomes. Execute tools and processes that improve master data management operations execution.

  • Collaborate on the preparation of metrics, dashboards, and tracking to facilitate the efficient operations of the department. 
  • Participate in, and sometimes lead, operational and governance meetings including end-to-end, value capture, and third-party reviews.  
  • Collaborate to create and maintain documentation for area of responsibility. May include Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), Work Instructions (WIs), protocols, data quality catalogs, and other policy and procedure documents. Deliver training to educate partners on detailed procedures, data flows, etc. 
  • Recommend creative solutions and participate in exploring refinement and implementation of those solutions. 
  • Support the deployment of the Data Management Framework 

Qualifications:  

Education

  • Minimum of a Bachelor’s or equivalent University Degree required

 

Required Knowledge, Skills and Abilities:  

  • A minimum of 2 years of relevant professional business experience is required.  
  • Strong experience, preferably within the health care or FMCG industry.
  • data processing systems (e.g. SAP required, OMP+ preferred)
  • procedures & Tools for processing and managing Master Data
  • GxP requirements
  • Basic Business and/or technical knowledge and understanding of Supply Chain data elements, process, and organizational support models.
  • MS Office Suite (Excel, Outlook, Word). 
  • Strong written, oral, and presentation skills. 
  • Strong organizational and prioritization skills. 
  • Ability to build strong collaborative relationships, influence others within job area through explanation of facts, policies and practices 
  • Ability to contribute on a diverse, multicultural and remote team. 
  • Ability to prioritize multiple initiatives within timelines, in a matrix environment where priorities change rapidly.  
  • English fluently required, local language skills strongly preferred
  • This position may require up to 5% of domestic and international (US) travel.  
  • Supervisor/people management experience is not required.

Key Working Relationships: 

  • Internal: IMSC MDM team, Value Chain Management roles eg. Supply Chain development leads, Planning functions such as E2E Planning, Site Planning, Finance, MAKE Operations, Quality assurance 
  • External: Third party suppliers of operational and/or project functions. 

Other

  • May require up to 10% domestic and/or international travel to other locations and sites

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Don’t settle for just any consulting company, choose the one that’s right for you!




At Data Wizards, we value perspeverance, curiosity, creativity, integrity and excellence. In that sense, we are looking for passionate AI & data professionals who want to help companies navigate the transition the world is facing today, and who want to be part of the Wizard adventure we are building.




On our side, we do our best to find exciting, challenging and meaningful projects for you. Today, we work with companies of all sizes, across various sectors, where we believe our work brings real value and purpose.




We are continuously on the lookout for bright data engineers, data scientists, machine learning engineers, AI engineers, and data analysts. But this job description is for Data Analysts out there feeling the magic in them and ready to spread it.







As a Data Analyst, your project would typically include:

Collecting, cleaning, and transforming data from various sources to ensure quality and usability
Writing and optimizing SQL queries and/or using Python to analyze and process data
Building and maintaining dashboards and reports (e.g. Power BI) to support business decision-making
Translating business needs into data requirements and actionable insights
Performing exploratory data analysis to identify trends, patterns, and opportunities
Collaborating with data engineers and other teams to ensure data availability and reliability
Contributing to the improvement of data models, datasets, and reporting processes
Ensuring data accuracy, consistency, and documentation of your work
Supporting stakeholders by explaining insights in a clear and structured way




You’re a step closer to joining our team if you bring:




Background:

Masters degree related to computer science, data or AI
1-4 years of experience in a data role
Previous experience building or maintaining data pipelines and ETL/ELT processes.
Exposure to working with large datasets from multiple sources (e.g., CRM, web analytics, call logs, transactional systems).

Technical Skills:

Solid hands-on experience with Python, and SQL.
Experience with SAS is a plus.
Experience with Databricks is a plus.
Familiarity with data warehousing concepts and best practices.
Experience integrating reporting tools such as Power BI.
Basic understanding of version control (e.g., Git) and automated workflows/scheduling.

Soft Skills:

Strong problem-solving and analytical thinking skills.
Ability to collaborate effectively with technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Good communication skills and attention to detail.
Proactive mindset with a willingness to learn and grow in a fast-moving environment.




Practical Info:

Languages: Native in French or Dutch and fluent in English




We know that finding the right job isn’t just about the role, it’s about what’s in it for you, too. While we’re a start-up, we’re big on taking care of our people right from day one.




Here’s a sneak peek at what we offer:




💰 A compensation package that keeps you motivated

Competitive salary
Mobility budget or company car with a fuel card
30 days of paid time off
A top-tier laptop & smartphone with a subscription
Representation allowances
Hospitalization & dental insurance
Meal vouchers
Opportunity to earn company stock over time




🎯 A place to be your best self, grow, and thrive

Work with the most innovative technologies in Data & AI
Work in an agile, collaborative environment
Hybrid work model
Monthly team-building events (plus one epic annual celebration)
Tailored training programs to fuel your career growth
A cool, ergonomic office with creative meeting spaces and a 24/7 fully equiped gym
A Nintendo Switch for spontaneous Mario Kart battles 🎮
A massage chair for those deep-thinking moments 🧘
Fresh coffee & drinks to keep you energized ☕




✨ And most importantly, a growing company where opportunities are limitless!




Our Recruitment Process:

Quick intro call with our HR team
HR Interview
Technical Interview
Partner Meet-up
Potential Interview with a client




At Data Wizards, we value perspeverance, curiosity, creativity, integrity and excellence. In that sense, we are looking for passionate AI & data professionals who want to help companies navigate the transition the world is facing today, and who want to be part of the Wizard adventure we are building.




If you’re passionate about what we do but aren’t sure you meet every single requirement, don’t hesitate to apply. We value potential, mindset, and growth as much as experience. At Data Wizards, we believe diversity is our strength. We welcome candidates from all backgrounds and experiences because different viewpoints help us grow and evolve.




Let’s build the future of AI & Data, together🤝




Please note: As a consulting company, our recruitment process can be either project-driven or based on strong profiles we believe in. The consultancy world moves fast, and while we always aim to match great talent with the right opportunity, there might not always be an immediate fit. With your permission, we’ll gladly keep your profile in mind for future missions, and hope to collaborate with you soon on an exciting project at Data Wizards!

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Wat je ambities ook zijn: TriFinance geeft je alle ruimte om ze waar te maken. Hier werk je elke dag aan projecten die passen bij jouw groeiplannen. Bovendien blijf je stappen vooruit zetten in een omgeving die gericht is op actieve kennisdeling, coaching en mentoring. Kortom: wij zorgen voor kansen, jij toont de ambitie om ze te grijpen. Klinkt goed, zeg je? Dan is het alvast onze ambitie om jou te ontmoeten.




Jouw uitdaging

Je combineert diverse data engineering projecten in verschillende sectoren
Je pakt complexe en analytische problemen aan als data analyst of data engineer
Je ondersteunt klanten in het ontwerpen en implementeren van een moderne Data Warehouse Architectuur




Jouw profiel

Je hebt een bachelor of een master diploma gecombineerd met relevante ervaring als data engineer / data analyst in Microsoft BI & Analytics (3 tot 7 jaar)
Je hebt kennis van moderne DW concepten, processen en methodologie, kennis van AI en machine learning is een troef
Je beschikt over technische en functionele kennis binnen één van volgende domeinen van Azure data & analytics technology stack: Azure Data Lake, Azure Data Factory, Azure SQL, Azure Machine learning, Databricks, Power BI, Fabric, …
Kennis van Python is een plus
Je hebt ervaring in het opzetten van data warehouses en / of lakehouses, dimensionele rapportering modellen en/of in AI en machine learning modellen
Je bent een geboren teamplayer en je kan tegelijk zelfstandig werken
Je spreekt vlot Nederlands en Engels, kennis van het Frans is een plus
Flexibiliteit is key! Met projecten verspreid over Vlaanderen, zoeken we samen naar de beste match, zowel op vlak van inhoud als op vlak van locatie. Daarin waarderen we je flexibele houding en kijken we samen naar oplossingen. Hybride werken bespreken wij ook altijd met onze klanten.




Ons aanbod

Vraag het maar aan onze mensen: bij TriFinance draait alles om jouw groei. We bekijken dan ook graag met jou welke richting jij uit wil gaan. Bovendien investeren we volop in inspirerende opleidingen, actieve coaching en leuke events onder collega’s. Dat alles in een open cultuur waar TriFinance-collega’s hun kennis delen en elkaar vooruithelpen. Verder kun je rekenen op een bijzonder veelzijdig loonpakket met naast een competitief salaris ook:

een bedrijfswagen (Mini/BMW) met tankkaart
een smartphone met abonnement
een netto onkostenvergoeding
maaltijd- en ecocheques
een groeps- en hospitalisatieverzekering
flexibele werkuren
32 vakantiedagen
een aantrekkelijk cafetariaplan.




Over TriFinance

We zijn trots om te delen dat TriFinance officieel ‘Great Place To Work’ gecertificeerd is, en erkend wordt als een Best Workplace! Maar liefst 91% van onze collega’s beschouwt TriFinance als een geweldige werkplek en 96% voelde zich meteen welkom bij ons bedrijf. Deze resultaten weerspiegelen het belang dat we hechten aan een positieve werkomgeving die het beste in iedereen naar boven haalt!




TriFinance is een internationale netwerkorganisatie en een unieke dienstverlener met een focus op Finance & Advisory. We bieden pragmatisch advies en operationele ondersteuning door middel van projectsourcing en werving- en selectiediensten. In 2022 hebben we ons dienstenportfolio uitgebreid met de introductie van TriHD.




Sinds onze start in 2002, staan onze medewerkers op de eerste plaats. Deze aanpak onderscheidt ons van andere consultancybureaus. Door ons samen te focussen op persoonlijke en professionele groei, zorgen we dat de best mogelijke kwaliteit in Do-How service wordt geleverd aan onze klanten. Solliciteer nu en groei samen mee met onze TriFamily met +900 collega’s!

Title: Business Intelligence (BI) Developer
Description:
Location: any site across United Kingdom or Benelux

At Yusen Logistics, we are working every day towards our ambition of becoming the world’s preferred supply chain logistics company. Through our expertise in freight forwarding, warehousing, transportation and supply chain management, we connect businesses, markets and communities around the globe.

With this in mind we’re seeking to grow our Data Team with the appointment of two Business Intelligence (BI) Developers to support our Healthcare+ business!

Your role

As BI Developer you will design, build and support business intelligence solutions that enable data-driven decision-making across Yusen Healthcare. You will work closely with business stakeholders to understand reporting and insight requirements, translating these into reliable dashboards, reports and analytical outputs. The role has end-to-end responsibility for BI solutions, including development, delivery and ongoing support.

In addition to BI delivery, the role may contribute to data engineering activities where required, including supporting data pipelines, transformations and datasets that underpin BI solutions. The role also contributes to the ongoing improvement of BI and data practices, with alignment to wider Yusen data approaches over time.

This position reports to the Data Solutions Manager, Yusen Logistics.

Your responsibilities include:


Design, build and support business intelligence solutions, including dashboards, reports and analytical datasets, that enable data driven decision making.
Gather, prepare and transform data from internal and external sources to support BI and reporting requirements.
Analyse data to identify trends, patterns and performance drivers, translating these into clear and actionable insights.
Work closely with business stakeholders to understand reporting and insight needs, translating requirements into effective BI solutions.
Support data pipelines, transformations and datasets that underpin BI solutions, contributing to data engineering activities where required.
Deliver BI and related data changes through structured, backlog driven delivery, balancing new development with BAU support.
Apply data quality checks and validation, and document BI solutions, data logic and assumptions to support ongoing use and support.
Contribute to continuous improvement of BI solutions, data processes and ways of working.



Your profile:


2–4 years’ experience in a BI Developer, Data Analyst or data-focused reporting role, delivering dashboards, reports and analytical outputs in a business environment.
Strong understanding of business intelligence and reporting concepts, including data modelling for analytics.
Proficiency in SQL for querying, transforming and analysing data and experience working with structured data.
Hands-on experience developing BI solutions using Power BI, including dashboards and datasets.
Familiarity with data integration, data cleansing and validation concepts.

HTML:
Location: any site across United Kingdom or Benelux

At Yusen Logistics, we are working every day towards our ambition of becoming the world’s preferred supply chain logistics company. Through our expertise in freight forwarding, warehousing, transportation and supply chain management, we connect businesses, markets and communities around the globe.

With this in mind we’re seeking to grow our Data Team with the appointment of two Business Intelligence (BI) Developers to support our Healthcare+ business!

Your role

As BI Developer you will design, build and support business intelligence solutions that enable data-driven decision-making across Yusen Healthcare. You will work closely with business stakeholders to understand reporting and insight requirements, translating these into reliable dashboards, reports and analytical outputs. The role has end-to-end responsibility for BI solutions, including development, delivery and ongoing support.

In addition to BI delivery, the role may contribute to data engineering activities where required, including supporting data pipelines, transformations and datasets that underpin BI solutions. The role also contributes to the ongoing improvement of BI and data practices, with alignment to wider Yusen data approaches over time.

This position reports to the Data Solutions Manager, Yusen Logistics.

Your responsibilities include:

  • Design, build and support business intelligence solutions, including dashboards, reports and analytical datasets, that enable data driven decision making.
  • Gather, prepare and transform data from internal and external sources to support BI and reporting requirements.
  • Analyse data to identify trends, patterns and performance drivers, translating these into clear and actionable insights.
  • Work closely with business stakeholders to understand reporting and insight needs, translating requirements into effective BI solutions.
  • Support data pipelines, transformations and datasets that underpin BI solutions, contributing to data engineering activities where required.
  • Deliver BI and related data changes through structured, backlog driven delivery, balancing new development with BAU support.
  • Apply data quality checks and validation, and document BI solutions, data logic and assumptions to support ongoing use and support.
  • Contribute to continuous improvement of BI solutions, data processes and ways of working.


Your profile:

  • 2–4 years’ experience in a BI Developer, Data Analyst or data-focused reporting role, delivering dashboards, reports and analytical outputs in a business environment.
  • Strong understanding of business intelligence and reporting concepts, including data modelling for analytics.
  • Proficiency in SQL for querying, transforming and analysing data and experience working with structured data.
  • Hands-on experience developing BI solutions using Power BI, including dashboards and datasets.
  • Familiarity with data integration, data cleansing and validation concepts.
  • Working knowledge of data pipelines and how BI tools consume and depend on underlying data.
  • Experience contributing to data preparation, transformation or modelling activities that support BI solutions.
  • Experience supporting BI solutions in production, including issue investigation and enhancement.
  • Experience working in iterative, delivery-focused teams (e.g. agile or sprint-based delivery).
  • Experience working with Microsoft Azure-based data platforms and BI tools such as Databricks and Fabric/Power BI.
  • Fluent proficiency in English (written and spoken).


We offer you:

  • The opportunity to contribute to the business intelligence solutions of tomorrow's healthcare supply chains
  • Collaboration with international teams and experienced BI and data professionals
  • Opportunities for continuous learning, certifications and career development
  • A competitive salary and attractive benefits package

Apply now and help us power the business intelligence solutions behind global healthcare logistics!

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
WHAT IS CREAM ALL ABOUT?

We could be a Cream Factory, a cooking blog or even a detective agency…

But in reality, we are a Business & Technology consulting company, active in large organizations and industry leaders in Banking, Insurance, Telecom, Energy, & Transport.

The Cream recipe

• 1/3 Personal development • 1/3 Collective intelligence • 1/3 Intrapreneurship • Bonus ingredient: some passionate colleagues

YOUR FUTURE PROJECTS :

Like Benjamin, Data Analyst at Cream, you work close to business teams and help them turn data into concrete insights that support decision-making. Your tasks will be :

• Understand business needs and translate them into analytical questions

• Collect, clean and analyse large datasets

• Identify trends, patterns and performance drivers

• Build dashboards, reports and analytical outputs

• Support business users in data interpretation

• Contribute to the selection and use of analytics tools

• Work closely with business, IT and data teams

WHAT DO WE EXPECT FROM OUR CONSULTANT ?

• At least 2 years of experience in data analysis AND Master’s degree

• Experience with SQL and BI tools (Power BI, Qlik, Tableau, …)

• Knowledge of Python or SAS

• Fluent in French and English; a good level of Dutch is an asset

A consulting company of around 200 Creamers, located in the center of Brussels




Our recruitment process is clear and structured, with three steps and feedback throughout.

Cream Consulting is committed to equal opportunities. If you need any specific accommodation to fully participate in the recruitment process or the interview, please let us know.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Bij Christeyns zetten we data om in waardevolle inzichten. Als internationale B2B-speler in de hygiënechemie investeren we sterk in een toekomstgerichte data-omgeving die onze bedrijfsprocessen en duurzaamheidsambities ondersteunt.

Om dit verder uit te bouwen, zoeken we een Data Scientist die complexe data vertaalt naar heldere analyses, dashboards en modellen met directe impact op besluitvorming en rapportering.




Jouw functie:

Je vertaalt ruwe en complexe data uit verschillende interne en externe bronnen naar heldere, bruikbare inzichten voor proceseigenaars en management, zodat beslissingen onderbouwd en datagedreven kunnen worden genomen.
Je ontwerpt, ontwikkelt en onderhoudt dashboards, datamodellen en API’s die bedrijfsprocessen meetbaar, stuurbaar en transparant maken binnen een internationale organisatie.
Je werkt actief mee aan de verdere uitbouw en optimalisatie van de data-architectuur en -infrastructuur, waarbij betrouwbaarheid, performantie en schaalbaarheid centraal staan.
Je ontsluit data via gebruiksvriendelijke visualisaties en rapporteringen in tools zoals Power BI en Grafana, ondersteund door Databricks en Microsoft Azure.
Je neemt verantwoordelijkheid voor duurzaamheids- en ESG-gerelateerde data en zorgt voor correcte definities, consistente datasets en duidelijke rapportering ter ondersteuning van strategische doelstellingen.
Waar relevant pas je machine learning en geavanceerde analysetechnieken toe om patronen te identificeren, inzichten te verdiepen en data verder te verrijken.
Je stemt noden en verwachtingen rond data, visualisatie en voorspellende modellen af met proceseigenaars en werkt hiervoor nauw samen met IT en het data team.
Je rapporteert aan de Data Team Lead




Jouw profiel:

Master of Bachelor in Data Science, Computerwetenschappen, (Industrieel) Ingenieur, Fysica, Economie of gelijkwaardig door ervaring
Ervaring met Python, machine learning, SQL Server, Power BI en Microsoft Azure
Sterk analytisch vermogen met een uitgesproken interesse in statistiek en technologie
Kennis van open source software en ERP-systemen
Communicatief sterk en in staat om complexe data helder te vertalen naar inzichten
Zeer goede communicatieve vaardigheden in Nederlands en Engels




Ons aanbod:

Een impactvolle functie bij Christeyns, een internationale B2B-speler in de hygiënechemie
Werken in een jong en dynamisch data team
Ruimte voor opleiding en persoonlijke groei
Een marktconform salarispakket met extralegale voordelen




Wie zijn wij:

Christeyns is een snelgroeiende Belgische hygiënespecialist in het B2B-segment met hoofdzetel in Gent. In iets meer dan 79 jaar evolueerde het bedrijf van een lokaal familiebedrijf tot een gerenommeerde wereldspeler in de internationale markt van chemicaliën en detergenten, met tevreden klanten in meer dan 50 landen. De verschillende domeinen van de bedrijfsactiviteiten omvatten professionele textielverzorging, de voedings- en drankenindustrie, professionele reiniging, life sciences en gezondheidszorg. Christeyns onderscheidt zich vooral door een permanente focus op klantgerichtheid, innovatie en ontwikkeling. De meer dan 1.000 medewerkers zijn verspreid over meer dan 44 dochterondernemingen op alle continenten en dragen bij aan een geconsolideerde omzet van meer dan 445 miljoen euro in 2024.

Meer informatie is beschikbaar op www.christeyns.com.




Interesse?

Klaar om als Data Scientist mee te bouwen aan een schonere, veiligere wereld?

Stuur je cv en motivatie naar jobs@christeyns.com.

Title: Analyst, Data Operations
Description:
At Johnson & Johnson, we believe health is everything. Our strength in healthcare innovation empowers us to build a world where complex diseases are prevented, treated, and cured, where treatments are smarter and less invasive, and solutions are personal. Through our expertise in Innovative Medicine and MedTech, we are uniquely positioned to innovate across the full spectrum of healthcare solutions today to deliver the breakthroughs of tomorrow, and profoundly impact health for humanity. Learn more at jnj.com.

As guided by Our Credo, Johnson & Johnson is responsible to our employees who work with us throughout the world. We provide an inclusive work environment where each person is considered as an individual. At Johnson & Johnson, we respect the diversity and dignity of our employees and recognize their merit.

Job Function

Data Analytics & Computational Sciences

Job Sub Function

Business Intelligence

Job Category

Professional

All Job Posting Locations:

Beerse, Antwerp, Belgium, Latina, Italy, Leiden, South Holland, Netherlands, Ringaskiddy, Cork, Ireland, Schaffhausen, Switzerland

Job Description

At Johnson & Johnson, we believe health is everything. Our strength in healthcare innovation empowers us to build a world where complex diseases are prevented, treated, and cured, where treatments are smarter and less invasive, and solutions are personal. Through our expertise in Innovative Medicine and MedTech, we are uniquely positioned to innovate across the full spectrum of healthcare solutions today to deliver the breakthroughs of tomorrow, and profoundly impact health for humanity. Learn more at https://www.jnj.com/

  

As we are building out our Data Strategy and Operations organization as part of our Value Chain Management organization in the Innovative Medicine Supply Chain we are recruiting for this role as part of our Master Data Operations team located in in either Titusville, NJ; Raritan, NJ; Zug, CH; Schaffhausen, CH; Leiden, NL; Cork, IE; Latina, IT or Beerse, BE. 

Please note that this role is available across multiple countries and may be posted under different requisition numbers to comply with local requirements. While you are welcome to apply to any or all of the postings, we recommend focusing on the specific country(s) that align with your preferred location(s):

United States - Req#: R-079226

EU - Req# R-082185

Remember, whether you apply to one or all of these requisition numbers, your applications will be considered as a single submission.

The IMSC MDM Operations team delivers trusted data to enable the launch & supply of products to our customers. Our key accountabilities include governance and maintenance of the Master Data Operations Framework; delivering a regulated and compliant data environment; using intelligent automation, workflow, process improvements, and third-party suppliers to improve quality, timeliness, cost, and effort across Supply Chain; applying deep end-to-end knowledge and subject matter expertise to define, shape and implement supply chain strategies; and cultivating talent as a distinctive capability. 

This position will execute day-to-day master data management operations. Will develop solutions to a variety of problems of moderate scope and complexity, while referring to established policies and procedures. 

Major Duties & Responsibilities: 

Responsible for the timely, efficient and accurate coordination, creation and maintenance of various master data elements (e.g. Material Master, BOM/Recipes, Production Versions,etc.) in SAP/ECC directly and/or through automated workflow system(s) and the execution of impact analyses or feasibility studies of change requests so that material planning, production, release, finance or other processes can proceed optimally.

Execute the data operations and data quality process. May include coordination of testing, error handling, data conversion and/or validation, and audit/compliance actions (Non-Conformance (NC) and/or Corrective Action Preventive Action (CAPA)).

Engage with partners on tactical matters. Provide tactical guidance and knowledge transfer to third-party team(s) and serve as point of escalation.

Participate in the completion of projects and departmental goals by tactically focusing on business outcomes. Execute tools and processes that improve master data management operations execution.


Collaborate on the preparation of metrics, dashboards, and tracking to facilitate the efficient operations of the department. 
Participate in, and sometimes lead, operational and governance meetings including end-to-end, value capture, and third-party reviews.  
Collaborate to create and maintain documentation for area of responsibility. May include Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), Work Instructions (WIs), protocols, data quality catalogs, and other policy and procedure documents. Deliver training to educate partners on detailed procedures, data flows, etc. 
Recommend creative solutions and participate in exploring refinement and implementation of those solutions. 
Support the deployment of the Data Management Framework 


Qualifications:  

Education


Minimum of a Bachelor’s or equivalent University Degree required


 

Required Knowledge, Skills and Abilities:  


A minimum of 2 years of relevant professional business experience is required.  
Strong experience, preferably within the health care or FMCG industry.
data processing systems (e.g. SAP required, OMP+ preferred)
procedures & Tools for processing and managing Master Data
GxP requirements
Basic Business and/or technical knowledge and understanding of Supply Chain data elements, process, and organizational support models.
MS Office Suite (Excel, Outlook, Word). 
Strong written, oral, and presentation skills. 
Strong organizational and prioritization skills. 
Ability to build strong collaborative relationships, influence others within job area through explanation of facts, policies and practices 
Ability to contribute on a diverse, multicultural and remote team. 
Ability to prioritize multiple initiatives within timelines, in a matrix environment where priorities change rapidly.  
English fluently required, local language skills strongly preferred
This position may require up to 5% of domestic and international (US) travel.  
Supervisor/people management experience is not required.


Key Working Relationships: 


Internal: IMSC MDM team, Value Chain Management roles eg. Supply Chain development leads, Planning functions such as E2E Planning, Site Planning, Finance, MAKE Operations, Quality assurance 
External: Third party suppliers of operational and/or project functions. 


Other


May require up to 10% domestic and/or international travel to other locations and sites


Johnson & Johnson is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, national origin, disability, protected veteran status or other characteristics protected by federal, state or local law. We actively seek qualified candidates who are protected veterans and individuals with disabilities as defined under VEVRAA and Section 503 of the Rehabilitation Act.

Johnson & Johnson is committed to providing an interview process that is inclusive of our applicants’ needs. If you are an individual with a disability and would like to request an accommodation, external applicants please contact us via https://www.jnj.com/contact-us/careers, internal employees contact AskGS to be directed to your accommodation resource.

Required Skills

Preferred Skills:

Advanced Analytics, Analytical Reasoning, Business Behavior, Business Intelligence (BI), Data Analysis, Data Capturing, Data-Driven Decision Making, Data Modeling, Data Privacy Standards, Data Reporting, Detail-Oriented, Execution Focus, Process Oriented, Project Support, Requirements Analysis, Statistical Analysis Systems (SAS) Programming, Workflow Analysis

The anticipated pay range for this position, in the primary posting location, is:

The anticipated pay ranges for additional locations are:

Belgium – The anticipated base pay range for this position is 52,000 – 82,685

Switzerland – The anticipated base pay range for this position is 81.000 – 131,560

Ireland – The anticipated base pay range for this position is 42,400 – 67,965

Italy – The anticipated base pay range for this position is 33,700 – 54395

Benefits

In addition to base pay, we offer the following benefits*: an annual bonus with set target (% of pay) depending on pay grade / location, where the actual amount is based on the employees’ and companies’ performance of the previous calendar year, or sales commissions. Moreover, we offer vacation days, parental leave for a minimum of 12 weeks, bereavement leave, caregiver leave, volunteer leave, well-being reimbursement, programs for financial, physical and mental health. We also offer service anniversary and recognition awards, and subject to the terms of their respective plans, employees - and in some location’s eligible dependents - can participate in several insurance plans. For more information, visit Employee benefits | Supporting well-being & career growth | Johnson & Johnson Careers.


This is for informative purposes only. Amounts and actual benefits may vary by location and are subject to change.

Advanced Analytics, Analytical Reasoning, Business Behavior, Business Intelligence (BI), Data Analysis, Data Capturing, Data-Driven Decision Making, Data Modeling, Data Privacy Standards, Data Reporting, Detail-Oriented, Execution Focus, Process Oriented, Project Support, Requirements Analysis, Statistical Analysis Systems (SAS) Programming, Workflow Analysis

Title: Data Analyst - Wyre (Joint Venture Telenet x Fluvius)
Description:
Job Description

Welcome to Wyre, a brand-new infrastructure company in the market with the mission of successfully rolling out the network of the future. Wyre made its debut on July 1, 2023, and was born from a powerful collaboration between two leading companies: Telenet and Fluvius.

At Wyre, we aim to create an inclusive environment where everyone feels welcome, regardless of background, gender, ethnicity, or any disabilities. We are proud of our identity in which our corporate values – Be Ambitious, Be Brave, Be Real & Be One – are fully embraced. These values are the result of teamwork and guide us in achieving our goals and strengthening our culture.

Since personal growth is actively encouraged within Wyre, we are ready to support you in reaching your full potential through internal career opportunities and a wide range of learning opportunities. If you are looking for an exciting and challenging environment where you can contribute to a growth story, then Wyre is the place for you.

Working In The Data & Insights Team

The Data & Insights team is the beating heart of Wyre, enabling the organization to steer daily operational and business activities by guiding, ensuring, improving, and standardizing projects, processes, and changes in tools. The Data & Insights team, also part of BNT, shapes Wyre's data strategy and architecture and helps the business make informed, data-driven decisions by providing analytics, actionable insights and reporting. 

The Data & Insights team is looking for a Data Analyst to strengthen its team.

Working as a Data Analyst

As a Data Analyst, you support the business with a wide range of data-related questions and help translate insights into concrete actions. You work closely with various departments, ensuring they can rely on clear analyses, reliable reporting, and well-founded recommendations.

You provide support for ad hoc requests, responding quickly and accurately to specific information needs. At the same time, you contribute to building structural, long-term solutions that strengthen the organization’s data maturity.

In addition, you play an important role within the Data & Insights team itself. You contribute to the further development and optimization of data models, dashboards, definitions, and processes, helping to safeguard the “single source of truth.” In this way, you combine operational support with strategic impact and actively contribute to building a future-oriented data organization.

Your Responsibilities


You gather, query, clean, validate, and manipulate data from various sources, transforming it into actionable insights, reports, and dashboards.
You define, build, and automate dashboards relevant to the topics you work on, ensuring they deliver value and clarity.
You develop proactive partnerships with stakeholders, ensuring data is leveraged as efficiently and effectively as possible to meet their needs.
You conduct ad-hoc analyses while contributing to long-term strategic initiatives.
You actively promote the implementation of data-driven solutions and help elevate data skills and maturity within the organization.
You collaborate closely with data engineers and analytics engineers and actively contribute to Wyre’s data landscape.
You play an active role in the data community within Wyre, contributing through knowledge sharing, coaching, and inspiring others.


Our Ideal Candidate


You hold a master’s degree in a relevant field and have 3 to 5 years of experience as a data analyst or can demonstrate equivalent experience.
You communicate fluently in English, both verbally and in writing, and know how to adapt your message to your audience. Knowledge of Dutch is a plus.
You have proven experience with leading dashboarding tools such as Qlik Sense, Power BI, or Tableau. (Nice to have)
You have strong SQL skills (Must have). Experience with Snowflake or other cloud data warehouses is a strong asset. (Very nice to have)
Experience with DBT is a plus. (Nice to have)
You are familiar with common software engineering practices, such as version control (e.g., Git – nice to have).
You combine strong analytical skills with attention to detail, energy, and a positive, solution-oriented mindset.
You know how to bring business stakeholders along in your story and have a proven ability to strengthen a data-driven culture within the organization.
You are a strong communicator who can clearly translate complex topics for both technical and non-technical audiences.


What We Offer


You join a company that invests fully in you and optimizes the work environment according to your needs. This allows you to develop yourself while also maintaining a work-life balance.
You have full autonomy to further develop your expertise. We encourage your personal growth with internal mobility opportunities and extensive learning possibilities but also give you the chance to hit the pause button or explore a completely different path over time.
You enjoy the company of colleagues who are just as passionate as you are.  
You receive a competitive gross monthly salary depending on your experience, complemented by numerous fringe benefits.


Get Wyred, apply now!

Title: Data & AI Consultant
Description:
🚀 Rejoignez Idealis Consulting et boostez votre carrière !




Idealis Consulting, entreprise en pleine croissance, qui accompagne les organisations dans leur transformation digitale grâce à des solutions ERP innovantes (Odoo et SAP).

Ici, sens, talent et prise d’initiative sont au cœur de notre réussite : culture collaborative, projets à fort impact, proximité avec le management, autonomie et formations pour développer vos compétences. Si vous cherchez un environnement où vos idées comptent, vous êtes au bon endroit.




En tant que Data Analyst & AI Consultant, votre objectif est d'aider les entreprises à créer de la valeur à partir de leurs données en y intégrant une forte dimension analytique et des technologies d'Intelligence Artificielle (IA). Vous analysez les besoins des clients, vous êtes le responsable de l'identification et de l'implémentation des solutions appropriées, vous gérez plusieurs projets et vous êtes le point de support des utilisateurs finaux. Vous orchestrez l'ensemble de la chaîne de valeur des données : depuis l'acquisition et la centralisation des données provenant de diverses sources (Odoo et hors Odoo), jusqu'à leur manipulation, leur stockage et leur exploitation finale. Vous déployez des solutions de Business Intelligence, de Data Science et d'IA avancées. Vos interventions couvrent l'ensemble des niveaux d'analyse (descriptives, prédictives et génératives).




🎯 Vos responsabilités :




Analyse des besoins du client et cadrage:

Accompagnement des Sales lors des démo clients.
Prise des besoins du client lors de workshops dédiés à la Data et à l'IA.
Quantifier la charge de travail et les ressources appropriées pour mener à bien le projet.
Identifier rapidement les besoins-clef / besoins critiques du client.

Identification de l'infrastructure de la solution:

Design et identification du data pipeline complet (flux des données).
Déterminer, challenger et proposer les outils adaptés (ETL, Data Warehouse, Outils BI, architectures IA) en fonction des besoins, ressources et infrastructure du client.
Chercher l'inspiration dans d'autres secteurs et entreprises pour proposer des cas d'usage IA innovants.
Réalisation des plans d'action pour la solution utilisée.

Mise en place des solutions appropriées (BI, Data Science & IA):

Configuration de l'architecture et transformation des données afin de faciliter les analyses et les futurs rapports.
Confection de dashboards performants à partir d'outils BI (Power BI, Google Data Studio, ...).
Implémentation d'algorithmes en lien avec la data science et l'IA (Machine Learning, IA générative) afin d'automatiser, prédire ou classifier des processus business.

Accompagnement et formation des clients à la solution:

Préparer un support de formation adapté.
Donner des formations aux clients pour assurer l'adoption de l'outil.
Animer des séances de question-réponse.
Assurer le rôle de point de support pour les utilisateurs finaux.




👤 Votre profil :




Diplôme de Bachelor ou Master dans un domaine lié à la fonction (Data Science, Computer Science, Engineering/Business Engineering,..).
Minimum 3 ans d'expérience dans une fonction similaire de Data Analyst ou Consultant AI
Excellentes compétences en communication écrite et orale
Excellente maîtrise du français et de l'anglais avec une bonne connaissance du Néerlandais
Aisance générale avec les outils informatiques et maîtrise des méthodologies de gestion de projet.
Connaissance des solutions data traditionnelles (Database, Data Warehouse, ETL, outils BI).
Connaissance approfondie de SQL et du langage de programmation Python
Connaissance des statistiques, du machine learning et une maîtrise des technologies d'IA moderne (ex: LLMs, automatisation intelligente)
Orienté solutions , autonome et dynamique.
Excellente communication et esprit d'équipe.
Pragmatique et énergique, avec une attitude positive.
Curieux, avide d'apprendre et motivé par les résultats.
Capacité à faire facilement le lien entre l'aspect business et technique.




Nice to have




Maîtrise avancée du Dashboarding (PowerBI/Google DataStudio/...) et des outils Cloud autour de la BI (Data Warehouse, ETL, …)
Une connaissance de l'écosystème Odoo (et de la manière d'y coupler des solutions BI et IA).
Un passion pour l’intelligence artificielle et les nouvelles technologies
Certification outils BI




🚀 Ce que nous offrons :




Un environnement stimulant et innovant
Des formations continues, du coaching et un vrai soutien pour développer vos compétences
Des projets variés, tous secteurs confondus, avec des clients passionnants
De l’autonomie et la liberté d’expérimenter, proposer, améliorer
Une équipe bienveillante, motivée et toujours prête à s’entraider
Une ambiance dynamique, des événements réguliers et une réelle proximité avec le management
L’opportunité de grandir et d’avoir un impact concret sur la croissance d’Idealis




Vous êtes motivé(e) et prêt(e) à faire la différence ? Rejoignez-nous et participez à notre succès !

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
Als junior data analyst bij Hudson (Randstad Group) werk je op het snijvlak van data-analyse, procesoptimalisatie en strategische ondersteuning. Je verzamelt, structureert en analyseert grote hoeveelheden gegevens uit diverse bronnen en vertaalt deze naar heldere inzichten en visuele rapportages die onze interne en externe stakeholders ondersteunen in hun besluitvorming.







Jouw verantwoordelijkheden:




Verzamelen, structureren, verwerken en controleren van data uit diverse bronnen met behulp van gespecialiseerde tools, met oog voor kwaliteit en volledigheid.
Correleren van verschillende soorten data en herkennen van trends en patronen teneinde waardevolle inzichten te genereren, gerichte aanbevelingen te formuleren en voorspellende modellen en statistische analyses te ontwikkelen die bijdragen aan de dienstverlening.
Ontwikkelen van interactieve dashboards en visuele rapporten (o.a. via Power BI) waarin trends, knelpunten en opportuniteiten helder worden gevisualiseerd ter ondersteuning van besluitvorming.
Meewerken aan strategische rewardprojecten (zoals gender pay gap analyses, salarispredictie, simulatiefactoren,…) en deze prioriteiten omzetten in concrete datagedreven inzichten en oplossingen die inspelen op de noden van interne en externe stakeholders.
Optimaliseren van interne processen met focus op efficiëntie, schaalbaarheid en kwaliteit.
Automatiseren van repetitieve datamanipulaties (o.a. car mapping) via de ontwikkeling en implementatie van geavanceerde ML-algoritmen.
Verkennen van de businessvraag samen met de collega's data en engineering om de juiste vertaalslag te maken naar een effectieve en werkbare data-oplossing.
