Title: Product Data Analyst
Description:
The AI orchestration of your wildest imagination.

n8n is the open workflow orchestration platform built for the new era of AI. We give technical teams the freedom of code with the speed of no-code, so they can automate faster, smarter, and without limits. Backed by a fiercely inventive community and 500+ builder-approved integrations, we’re changing the way people bring systems together and scale ideas for impact.

Since our founding in 2019, we’ve grown into a diverse team of over 260 - working across Europe and the US, connected by a shared builder spirit and with our centre of gravity in Berlin. Along the way, we’ve:


Cultivated a community of more than 650,000 active developers and builders
Earned 190K+ GitHub stars, making us one of the world’s Top 40 most popular projects
Backed by top investors, from Sequoia’s first German seed to our SAP's recent strategic investment - bringing us to a $5.2bn valuation


That’s the company we’ve built. Now we’d love to see what you can build. If you’re applying, try n8n out - whether you’re technical or not - and share a screenshot of your first workflow with us. The easiest place to start is here: app.n8n.cloud/register.

We’re in a defining moment of an incredible journey. Come and build with us.

Join n8n as the dedicated data partner to a product team that's already hands-on with data.

Our PMs are technical and data-literate. They write SQL, run their own analyses, and know their corner of the product deeply. What they're missing is a dedicated analyst counterpart - someone who takes the data work further than they have time for, brings the outside perspective they can't get from inside their squad, and pairs with them on designing and improving the product.

That's the role. You'll be embedded with a product squad as the PMs' dedicated data partner, working on the deeper analyses, the cross-cutting views, and the questions that shape what gets built - with activation and adoption of n8n as your headline focus.

🎯 Your mission: be the dedicated data partner that helps a hands-on product team go deeper, see further, and design better products.

🤝 Be the PMs' data partner


Our PMs already do their own analyses. You're the counterpart who takes the work deeper and broader than they can in a sprint.
Take on the analyses they don't have time for, accelerate the ones they're running, and pair on the questions that matter most.
Free PMs to focus on building product by being the dedicated owner of the data work that supports it.


🔭 Bring perspective they can't get themselves


Cross-product cuts, segment views, comparative analyses across the user base — the outside lens that's hard to see from inside a single squad.
Challenge assumptions, including the ones PMs are most attached to.
Surface the analyses nobody asked for but everyone needed.


🚀 Help design and improve the product


Co-create hypotheses with PMs and pair on how to test them properly.
Activation and adoption are the headline: how users get to value, what drives lasting use, where we're losing them, and what to build to fix it.
Go deep on retention drivers, cohort dynamics, and the leading indicators of expansion. Connect feature-level analyses back to the bigger picture.


🗣 Influence the roadmap with a real POV


Sit in product planning as a peer. Bring strong, data-backed opinions about what we should build next — and what we shouldn't.
Push back constructively. Translate complex analysis into crisp recommendations that move decisions.


🧪 Raise the bar on rigor


Bring statistical depth where it matters and keep it out of the way when it doesn't.
Make experiments and analyses trustworthy — well-designed, properly powered, honestly read.
Partner with PMs and Engineering on event instrumentation so the data the team relies on is solid.


Requirements

Must-haves


📊 Senior product analytics chops: 5+ years analyzing product usage in B2B SaaS, ideally in a PLG environment.
🤝 Partner mindset: You thrive working alongside technical, hands-on PMs as a counterpart — making them better and being made better by them.
🚀 Activation & adoption expertise: You've owned the analysis behind onboarding funnels, time-to-value, feature adoption, and the behavioral signals that separate stuck users from successful ones.
🔎 Depth, not just breadth: You're known for going deeper than the obvious — behavioral segmentation, retention drivers, feature impact done properly.
🤓 Real applied statistics: Not "I took a stats class" — you can spot a biased metric, an underpowered test, or a confounder, and explain it clearly to a non-technical stakeholder.
🧭 Opinionated and high-agency: You don't wait for tickets. You see what's worth analyzing, you do it, and you bring a clear recommendation — even when the team didn't ask.
🧪 Solid experimentation skills: You can design and read A/B tests properly. It's one tool in your kit, not the only one.
🔧 Instrumentation fluency: You've defined event taxonomies and partnered with engineering on tracking specs.
💻 Technical toolkit: Expert SQL; comfort with modern stack (BigQuery, dbt, Metabase or equivalent).
🗣️ Communication that lands: You write memos PMs read end-to-end and present findings that change decisions.


Nice-to-haves


👾 Devtool / OSS / technical product experience: You get how technical audiences adopt and use products.
🐍 Python for analysis: pandas, statsmodels, scipy when SQL isn't enough.
🔗 Causal inference: For the questions A/B testing can't answer.
🧮 Experimentation platforms: GrowthBook, Statsig, Eppo, or similar.


Why join us?

At n8n, your work won't disappear into a slide deck. You'll pair with a technical, data-literate product team that already does its own analysis and genuinely values having a real analyst counterpart. You'll be embedded, trusted, and in the room where decisions get made — owning the questions that matter most for a fast-growing PLG product: how users activate, what drives adoption, and what keeps them coming back. You'll join a small, sharp data team with strong foundations already in place, inside one of Europe's fastest-growing open-source companies, at a moment where what we ship matters a lot.

Sound like a challenge you're ready to take on?

Apply now - and help us build the future of automation.

n8n is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, colour, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.

We can sponsor visas to Germany; for any other country, you need to have existing right to work.

Our company language is English.

You care about diversity and inclusion? We do too! Check out our Diversity, Inclusion and Belonging initiatives at n8n (https://www.notion.so/n8n/Diversity-inclusion-and-belonging-n8n-c1bec2fff536422d868b1a438d990e35).

Location disclaimer: If you see multiple job postings for the same role, it is most likely because we're hiring remotely for this role and posting in different locations to make sure every potential candidate can see the role. Please apply to the location you're the most likely to work from in the future.

Benefits


Competitive compensation 💸 – We offer fair and attractive pay.
Ownership 💪 – Our core value is to “empower others,” and we mean it—you’ll get a slice of n8n with equity.
Work/life balance 🏖️ – We work hard but ensure you have time to recharge:
Europe: 30 days of vacation, plus public holidays wherever you are.
US: 20 vacation days, 8 sick days, plus public holidays wherever you are.
Health & wellness 🩺 –
Europe: We provide benefits according to local country norms.*
US: Multiple low-premium, low-deductible medical plans with coverage for individuals and families—plus a no-cost premium HDHP option with a pre-seeded HSA—along with dental and vision coverage.
Future planning 💰 –
Europe: We provide pension contributions according to local country norms.*
US: 401(k) retirement plan with a 4% employer match.
Financial security 🛡️ –
Europe: We provide benefits according to local country norms.*
US: Company-paid short-term and long-term disability insurance, plus life insurance to support you and your loved ones.
Career growth 📈 – We hire rising stars who grow with us! You’ll get €1K (or equivalent) per year to spend on courses, books, events, or coaching to level up your skills.
A passionate team 🤩 – We love our product, and we prove it with regular hackathons where we see who can build the coolest thing with it!
Remote-first 🌏 – Our team works remotely across Europe, with regular off-sites for team bonding. Some roles, like sales in the US, are hybrid—please check the job description.
Giving back 🤝 – We're big fans of open source, and you'll get $100 per month to support projects you care about.
AI enablement 🤖 – We believe in working smarter—everyone gets an unlimited AI budget to explore and use the best tools to boost productivity and creativity.
Transparency 🙏 – We all know what everyone’s working on, how the company is doing—the whole shebang.
Country-specific details are provided in your contract.

Title: Stagiaire Data Analyst
Description:
Description de l'entreprise

Le présent et le futur d’Audemars Piguet se construisent grâce à la contribution de tous nos talents. Inspirés par la richesse de notre passé, nous sommes enthousiastes à l’idée de ce que nous créerons dans le futur. Ensemble, nous sommes résolument tournés vers l’avenir et en quête continue d’excellence dans tous nos métiers.

Si notre mission vous inspire, venez poursuivre votre parcours au sein de notre famille pour que l’on continue de créer l'extraordinaire. Écrivons le prochain chapitre de votre carrière ensemble !

Description du poste

Dans le cadre d’un projet stratégique de digitalisation de nos ateliers de production, nous proposons un stage de 6 mois, à partir de septembre 2026, centré sur la collecte et l’exploitation des données issues des moyens de mesure utilisés dans le flux de production horloger. Le projet s’inscrit au cœur de problématiques industrielles concrètes et aura un impact direct sur la robustesse et la performance de nos processus.

Dans ce cadre, vous serez en charge des principales tâches suivantes :


Structurer et consolider les données provenant de nos ateliers de production
Développer des outils analytiques permettant de créer de la valeur et de sécuriser notre production
Mettre en application ces outils au sein de notre production afin de proposer des réglages initiaux optimisés


Qualifications


Étudiant·e de Master en Data Science, Microtechnique, Génie mécanique, Informatique, ou domaine apparenté
Connaissances approfondies en programmation (Python ou MATLAB)
Maîtrise de MS office
Intérêt pour les systèmes de mesure et/ou la mécanique horlogère
Capacité à travailler de manière rigoureuse, structurée et méthodique
Esprit d’équipe, sens du terrain et motivation pour interagir de manière transverse

Title: IT & Market Data Procurement Analyst
Description:
Established in 2004, Mercuria is one of the leading integrated energy and commodity trading companies in the world. We bring energy markets together to support the needs of today by trading, structuring finance, and investing into strategic assets, while generating more than $110 billion in turnover.




Our operations span over 50 countries on 5 continents, including all the major energy hubs. We trade physical oil, energy products, environmental products and other commodities from Geneva, London, Singapore, Shanghai, Beijing, Dubai, Houston, Calgary and Greenwich (CT).




We are committed to advancing the transition to a more sustainable, affordable and reliable energy system for tomorrow. Over 50% of our assets are in low carbon & energy transition sectors - providing a strong platform to trade these new markets and support decarbonization.




In 2023, we established Silvania, a $500 million fund, investing into restoration and protection of nature and biodiversity globally and in support of the Paris Agreement goals & UN 30x30 biodiversity initiative. This new asset class supports nature protection and provides sustainable financial returns.










The role




This role sits within the CIO Office in Geneva and is responsible for end-to-end procurement, commercial management, and financial governance across Mercuria’s global technology estate, with a particular focus on IT services, software, and market data.

The position combines hands-on operational execution with commercial ownership and transformation, supporting IT leadership and senior business stakeholders to optimise technology spend, manage vendor relationships, and professionalise procurement processes across a fast-paced commodity trading environment.

The role actively promotes the adoption of data, automation, and AI-enabled capabilities within procurement activities

The role has global scope, high visibility, and regular exposure to senior management.










Key responsibilities




IT & Market Data Procurement

Manage the full procurement lifecycle for IT hardware, software, consultancy services, and market data
Support and challenge new technology spend, ensuring alignment with commercial best practice and governance
Act as a key interface between IT, business stakeholders, and external vendors




Market Data & Software Asset Management

Own software and market data subscriptions (licenses, renewals, usage, compliance)
Optimise costs through rationalisation and a strong understanding of vendor licensing models
Support evolving business needs (new desks, usage, trading activities)




Commercial & Contract Management

Manage IT contract renewals and vendor negotiations in collaboration with IT, Finance, and Legal
Maintain accurate contract records and strong vendor relationships
Monitor market trends and supplier roadmaps to anticipate risks and opportunities




Financial Management & Reporting

Track and analyse IT spend across software, hardware, consultancy, and market data
Support IT financial reporting, forecasting, and budget visibility
Perform spend analysis to identify trends, risks, and optimisation opportunities




Governance & Stakeholder Engagement

Prepare and support PMO, procurement, and governance forums
Produce high-quality reporting and materials for senior management and C-level audiences
Liaise effectively with global internal stakeholders and external suppliers
Promote strong procurement and compliance culture across the organisation.




Transformation & Continuous Improvement

Established in 2004, Mercuria is one of the leading integrated energy and commodity trading companies in the world. We bring energy markets together to support the needs of today by trading, structuring finance, and investing into strategic assets, while generating more than $110 billion in turnover.


Our operations span over 50 countries on 5 continents, including all the major energy hubs. We trade physical oil, energy products, environmental products and other commodities from Geneva, London, Singapore, Shanghai, Beijing, Dubai, Houston, Calgary and Greenwich (CT).


We are committed to advancing the transition to a more sustainable, affordable and reliable energy system for tomorrow. Over 50% of our assets are in low carbon & energy transition sectors - providing a strong platform to trade these new markets and support decarbonization.


In 2023, we established Silvania, a $500 million fund, investing into restoration and protection of nature and biodiversity globally and in support of the Paris Agreement goals & UN 30x30 biodiversity initiative. This new asset class supports nature protection and provides sustainable financial returns.




The role


This role sits within the CIO Office in Geneva and is responsible for end-to-end procurement, commercial management, and financial governance across Mercuria’s global technology estate, with a particular focus on IT services, software, and market data.

The position combines hands-on operational execution with commercial ownership and transformation, supporting IT leadership and senior business stakeholders to optimise technology spend, manage vendor relationships, and professionalise procurement processes across a fast-paced commodity trading environment.

The role actively promotes the adoption of data, automation, and AI-enabled capabilities within procurement activities

The role has global scope, high visibility, and regular exposure to senior management.

IT & Market Data Procurement

  • Support and challenge new technology spend, ensuring alignment with commercial best practice and governance

Commercial & Contract Management

  • Maintain accurate contract records and strong vendor relationships
  • Monitor market trends and supplier roadmaps to anticipate risks and opportunities


Financial Management & Reporting

  • Track and analyse IT spend across software, hardware, consultancy, and market data
  • Support IT financial reporting, forecasting, and budget visibility

Title: Reference Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description

Reference Data Analyst

______________________________________________________________________

Location: Zurich, Switzerland

Type: Full-time

Department: Financial Markets

_______________________________________________________________________

Summary

As a Reference Data Analyst you will be responsible for the accurate creation, maintenance, and governance of static and dynamic reference data across multiple financial products and systems.

This role plays a critical part in supporting collateral management operations by ensuring data integrity, timely updates, and alignment with regulatory and operational standards.

Responsibilities


Maintain and validate reference data for counterparties, securities, and agreements used in collateral management systems and workflows.
Set up accounts in Collateral Management Systems, ensuring alignment with CSAs, ISDA, and SMA agreements.
Collaborate with middle office, risk, and legal teams to onboard new counterparties, secure approvals and update records.
Liaise with external vendors (e.g., Bloomberg, Markit, DTCC) and internal stakeholders to source and verify data.
Initiate and coordinate SSIs setup requests and coordinate with Ops teams and stakeholders.
Address queries related to collateral accounts, SSIs setup.
Plan and track client onboarding schedules, highlighting dependencies and risks.
Maintain trackers and provide regular status updates in meetings and calls.
Ensure timely coordination and escalation of issues/risks.
Support additional or ad-hoc requests and projects.
Adhere to operational controls (e.g. SOPs, SLAs, and KPIs).
Participate in data quality initiatives, including exception reporting, root cause analysis, and remediation.
Contribute to automation and process improvement efforts using tools such as Alteryx, Xceptor, or internal workflow engines.
Ensure compliance with internal controls, audit requirements, and regulatory standards.


Eligibility Requirements


Ideally, 1–5 years of experience in reference data management, preferably within collateral, middle office, or securities operations.
Good understanding of financial instruments (e.g., OTC derivatives, equities, fixed income, FX) and collateral lifecycle.
Ideally, familiarity with collateral management platforms (e.g., AcadiaSoft, TriResolve, Murex) and data governance tools.
Proficiency in Excel (pivot tables, lookups), with exposure to SQL or data visualisation tools being a plus.
Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.
Ability to work under pressure and manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Preferred Qualifications
Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Economics, or a related field.
Knowledge of regulatory frameworks impacting collateral and data.
Experience with reconciliation tools and exception management workflows.
Languages
German and/or English
Work Permit
Must possess a Swiss Work Permit or a EU Passport

About EClerx

eClerx is a leading provider of productized services, bringing together people, technology and domain expertise to amplify business results.

The firm provides business process management, automation, and analytics services to a number of Fortune 2000 enterprises, including some of the world’s leading financial services, communications, retail, fashion, media & entertainment, manufacturing, travel & leisure, and technology companies. Incorporated in 2000, eClerx is traded on both the Bombay and National Stock Exchanges of India. The firm employs more than 19,000 people across Australia, Canada, France, Germany, Switzerland, Eqypt. India, Italy, Netherlands, Peru, Philippines, Singapore, Thailand, the UK, and the USA.

Title: Energy Data Portfolio Analyst – Fixed Term Contract
Description:
Contexte et environnement

This fixed-Term contract offers the opportunity to gain a deep understanding of the European energy market structure, collaborate with Front, Middle, Operations, and Back Office teams, and interact with external stakeholders such as system operators. You will strengthen your programming expertise across multiple languages and actively support the migration to next-generation tools and platforms.

We are committed to meeting the energy needs of a growing population and supporting the energy transition. The volatility of the energy trading markets requires excellence in risk management and a culture of innovation. We are fully embracing the digital revolution.

Our people flourish in an environment that promotes expertise, entrepreneurship spirit, agility and a purpose-driven culture, generating boundless opportunities to learn, grow and achieve collaborative success.

We look for passion, ambition and open-mindedness. While we evolve in a demanding industry, we cultivate a friendly workplace where our people feel good, where team spirit and respect guide our daily routine, where the diversity of our people and their skills create a nourishing experience for all of us.

This position operates within a continuously evolving European energy market and requires close coordination with multiple teams and geographies. It contributes to the company’s strategic objectives by supporting data‑driven decision‑making and operational reliability.

Activités

Over the past years, the team has automated most of its processes using Python, PowerQuery, Power BI, Databricks and Alteryx. The operational tasks of the EDM team involve:


Collecting volumetric power data from multiple external and internal sources and retrieving any missing volumes.
Ensuring data consistency by comparing current data with historical records and other sources but also by building some coherence checks to identify anomalies.
Injecting validated data into the internal database and producing key performance indicators (KPIs) to ensure data quality.



Your primary responsibilities will include maintaining and executing these operational tasks, as well as developing new controls to improve data reliability.

Profil du candidat


Educational standard required: Engineering or Finance background preferred.
Solid programming skills: Python experience required, proficiency in Excel, knowledge in PowerQuery, PowerBI, Databricks and Alteryx is an asset
Strong analytical mindset, attention to detail, and problem-solving ability
Collaborative team player who works well with internal team(s), partners and external stakeholders.
Proactive and well-organised, capable of working independently.
Fluency in English is required; proficiency in a second language (French, Spanish, German or Italian) is an asset.



Informations supplémentaires

TotalEnergies valorise la diversité, promeut le développement individuel et offre des opportunités d'emploi égales à tous les candidats.

Title: Controller & Data Analyst junior (m/f/x)
Description:
Your Responsibilities

As a Junior Controller & Data Analyst (m/f/x), you monitor expenses by cost center,
You analyze and comment on profit and loss variances (financial and analytical),
You support the preparation of budgets by cost center,
You participate in monthly closings as well as in the preparation of future plans,
You develop KPIs and performance BI reports/tools in collaboration with the company’s functional managers,
You carry out specific analyses and drive optimization initiatives,


Your Profil

You hold a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business Administration, Finance, or a related field,
You have at least 2 to 3 years of experience in controlling or data analysis, ideally in an industrial environment,
You are fluent in French and English and have good knowledge of German,
You are proficient in the MS Office suite and have solid knowledge of an ERP system,
You are autonomous, flexible, and able to work effectively within a team,
You stand out through your analytical skills, communication abilities, and your capacity to assert yourself.


Our Offer
Can we inspire you to take on a responsible position in a dynamic and independent family business? We offer you room for ideas, a secure job, modern and family-friendly employment conditions such as flexible working hours, home office, a company restaurant, training opportunities as well as many benefits. The workplace is located in Bulle, surrounded by the wonderful nature of the Gruyère region. Start planning your personal and professional development with us.
In order to ensure a fair application process, we cannot accept applications by post or email.
This position is subject to a background check process.

Have we awoken your interest? Then we look forward to receiving your online application. If you have any questions, please contact Nadege MALAN.
One Passion. Many Opportunities.

The Company
Liebherr Machines Bulle SA is the centre of excellence for combustion engines (diesel and gas engines) and hydraulic components (axial piston pumps and motors). The company develops and produces various products, which find their use in building construction and civil engineering, mining, agriculture, decentralised energy, machine and plant engineering, as well as aerospace.

Location
Liebherr Machines Bulle SA
7, Rue Hans-Liebherr
1630 Bulle
Switzerland (CH)

Contact

026 913 31 11

Title: Associate Director, Forecasting Data
Description:
Role Overview

The Data Leader – Forecasting will be responsible for building and governing high-quality, AI-ready data foundations to enable forecasting at scale. This role will drive the creation of certified data products and ensure data reliability, consistency, and accessibility across the enterprise.

Key Responsibilities

Data Strategy & Architecture


Define the data strategy for forecasting (demand, supply, inventory, pricing, external signals)
Design scalable data architecture on cloud platforms (e.g., GCP lakehouse)
Standardize data models and semantic layers across regions


Data Product Development


Build and own certified data products (forecast inputs, demand signals, master data)
Define SLAs, quality metrics, and certification standards
Enable self-service consumption for AI models and business users


Data Governance & Quality


Establish governance frameworks (ownership, lineage, cataloging, access control)
Ensure data quality, consistency, and completeness across multiple ERPs
Drive data readiness for AI use cases


Integration & Platform Enablement


Lead data ingestion and integration from internal systems and external sources
Collaborate with platform teams on pipelines, transformations, and storage
Ensure scalability and performance of data pipelines


Stakeholder Collaboration


Partner with AI teams, business stakeholders, and IT teams
Act as the bridge between business data needs and technical implementation
Drive data literacy and adoption across teams


Key Skills & Qualifications


10–15 years in data engineering, data architecture, or data platforms
Strong experience with cloud data platforms (GCP, Snowflake, Databricks)
Expertise in data modeling, ETL/ELT, and data governance
Experience working in multi-ERP, complex enterprise environments
Strong stakeholder management and communication skills


Success Metrics


Number of certified data products created
Data quality scores (accuracy, completeness, timeliness)
Time to enable new AI use cases
Reduction in data-related issues/escalations


Pay Range

CHF140,000.00 - CHF280,000.00

  • 10–15 years in data engineering, data architecture, or data platforms
  • Strong experience with cloud data platforms (GCP, Snowflake, Databricks)
  • Expertise in data modeling, ETL/ELT, and data governance
  • Experience working in multi-ERP, complex enterprise environments
  • Strong stakeholder management and communication skills

Success Metrics

  • Number of certified data products created
  • Data quality scores (accuracy, completeness, timeliness)
  • Time to enable new AI use cases
  • Reduction in data-related issues/escalations

Pay Range

CHF140,000.00 - CHF280,000.00

Title: MSAT Biologics Data Analyst
Description:
Make your mark for patients

We are looking for a MSAT Biologics Data Analyst who is curious, collaborative and proactive to join us in our Biologics Manufacturing Science and Technology team, based in Bulle, Switzerland.




About The Role

You will help improve efficiency and visibility across commercial manufacturing and investigation activities by automating data workflows and connecting data sources into one end-to-end view. You will build digital tools and prescriptive analytics to strengthen process performance by monitoring across sites, detect emerging risks and deviations earlier, and support data-driven decision-making during investigations, lifecycle management and continuous improvement.




Who You’ll Work With

You will be working in a team that supports biologics process teams across drug substance and drug product activities, partnering closely with colleagues involved in process investigations and ongoing improvements. You will also collaborate with information technology partners and data-focused roles across departments to share ideas, find synergies and co-develop solutions that can be reused across the organisation.




What You’ll Do

Create standard templates to capture process data consistently for manufacturing science and technology process teams.
Build lightweight automation tools that reduce repetitive manual data preparation using programming languages such as Python and R.
Analyse operational data and develop scripts that support performance tracking and continuous improvement.
Prototype algorithms using artificial intelligence, machine learning and natural language processing to support automation proofs of concept.
Automate pre-population of process and study protocols and reports using content management systems.
Create visual and analytical outputs that support surveillance, study oversight, risk trend detection and historical analysis and support adoption through training and guidance.




Interested?

For this role we’re looking for the following education, experience and skills

Master’s degree in a relevant quantitative or life-science field.
At least two years’ experience in data analysis, programming or automation in a life-sciences setting.
Proficiency in programming and automation (for example Python, R, or Statistical Analysis System software).
Ability to create data visualizations and dashboards (for example Qlik Sense or Microsoft Power BI).
Strong capability in working with databases and generating reports; able to manage multiple priorities.
Clear communicator who enjoys collaborating across cultures and time zones and can mentor colleagues when rolling out new tools.




Are you ready to ‘go beyond’ to create value and make your mark for patients? If this sounds like you, then we would love to hear from you!

About Us

UCB is a global biopharmaceutical company, focusing on neurology and immunology. We are over 9.000 people in all four corners of the globe, inspired by patients and driven by science.

Why work with us?

At UCB, we don’t just complete tasks, we create value. We aren’t afraid to push forward, collaborate, and innovate to make our mark for patients. We have a caring, supportive culture where everyone feels included, respected, and has equal opportunities to do their best work. We ‘go beyond’ to create value for our patients, and always with a human focus, whether that’s on our patients, our employees, or our planet. Working for us, you will discover a place where you can grow, and have the freedom to carve your own career path to achieve your full potential.

At UCB, we’ve embraced a hybrid-first approach to work, bringing teams together in local hubs to foster collaborative curiosity. Unless explicitly stated in the description or precluded by the nature of the position, roles are hybrid with 40% of your time spent in the office.

UCB is an equal opportunity employer. All employment decisions will be made without regard to any characteristic protected by applicable federal, state, or local law. UCB invites you to voluntarily self-identify during the application process. Provision of self-identification information is entirely voluntary and a decision to provide or not provide such information will not have any effect on your application for employment, your employment with UCB, or otherwise subject you to any adverse treatment. Any information you provide will be considered confidential and will be kept separate from your application and/or personnel file and will only be used in accordance with applicable laws, orders, and regulations.




Should you require any adjustments to our process to assist you in demonstrating your strengths

and capabilities contact us on US-Reasonable_Accommodation@ucb.com for application to US based roles. Please note should your enquiry not relate to adjustments; we will not be able to support you through this channel.

Title: Junior Media Insights Consultant, Audience and Market Insights, PFP, 12 months, Geneva, Switzerland (Remote)
Description:
UNICEF works in over 190 countries and territories to save children’s lives, defend their rights, and help them fulfill their potential, from early childhood through adolescence.

At UNICEF, we are committed, passionate, and proud of what we do for as long as we are needed. Promoting the rights of every child is not just a job – it is a calling.

UNICEF is a place where careers are built. We offer our staff diverse opportunities for professional and personal development that will help them reinforce a sense of purpose while serving children and communities across the world. We welcome everyone who wants to belong and grow in a diverse and passionate culture, coupled with an attractive compensation and benefits package.

Visit our website to learn more about what we do at UNICEF.

How can you make a difference? 

This role is being established to strengthen the capacity of the Audience and Market Insights (AMI) unit by bringing in dedicated media analysis expertise. The Junior Media Insights Consultant will be primarily responsible for supporting monitoring, tracking, and analyzing media coverage and performance data, while also contributing to broader desk research activities that support the team's insight outputs. The role suits an early-career analyst with a background in media monitoring, content analysis, or communications research.


Media Analysis and Landscape Mapping: Support the team with media analysis that informs strategic decisions on partnership development and channel prioritization. This includes conducting periodic media landscape analyses — mapping the broader media environment to surface shifts in topic prominence, emerging narratives, evolving editorial agendas, and how key themes are being covered across outlets and competitor platforms. Outputs will range from regular analysis of snapshots to deeper landscape reports, formatted for internal distribution across the AMI team, PFP division, and wider stakeholders.
Desk Research Support: Assist the AMI team on an ad hoc basis with secondary research tasks — including landscape reviews, competitor scans, and audience background research — contributing to the synthesis and write-up of insight reports as workload requires.
Data and Insights Organization: Maintain up-to-date insight databases, dashboards, and internal repositories (e.g., SharePoint, Teams folders, Boolean searches’ archive) to ensure research materials are easily accessible and systematically organized.
Contribution to Research Projects and Insight Reports: Support end-to-end research activities, including questionnaire design, data synthesis, and the creation of final insight reports for internal distribution.


If you would like to know more about this consultancy, please review the complete Terms of Reference here: TMC0002477 ToR.pdf

To qualify as an advocate for every child you will have:

Minimum Requirements


Education: Master’s degree in cultural studies, communications, psychology, marketing, political studies, development studies, or a related field.
Work Experience:
Minimum of 6 months of professional experience in market research, data analysis, or audience insights (agency or client-side).
Familiarity with quantitative and qualitative research methods.
Experience with digital and analytics tools (e.g., Brandwatch, Talkwalker, Google Analytics).
Language requirement: Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English
Skills: Strong analytical and data visualization skills (Excel, PowerPoint, Power BI, Tableau, or similar).
Strong attention to detail, organization, and ability to manage multiple tasks.
Curious and constructive attitude, with a desire to learn and grow in the craft of research and insights.

Desirables


Method neutrality is an asset [quantitative, qualitative, and secondary research]
Knowledge of additional UN languages is an asset


For every Child, you demonstrate...

UNICEF’s Core Values of Care, Respect, Integrity, Trust and Accountability and Sustainability (CRITAS) underpin everything we do and how we do it. Get acquainted with Our Values Charter: UNICEF Values

UNICEF promotes and advocates for the protection of the rights of every child, everywhere, in everything it does and is mandated to support the realization of the rights of every child, including those most disadvantaged, and our global workforce must reflect the diversity of those children. The UNICEF family is committed to include everyone, irrespective of their race/ethnicity, disability, gender identity, sexual orientation, religion, nationality, socio-economic background, minority, or any other status.

UNICEF encourages applications from all qualified candidates, regardless of gender, nationality, religious or ethnic backgrounds, and from people with disabilities, including neurodivergence. We offer reasonable accommodation for persons with disabilities. throughout the recruitment process. If you require any accommodation, please submit your request through the accessibility email button on the UNICEF Careers webpage Accessibility | UNICEF. Should you be shortlisted, please get in touch with the recruiter directly to share further details, enabling us to make the necessary arrangements in advance.

UNICEF does not hire candidates who are married to children (persons under 18). UNICEF has a zero-tolerance policy on conduct that is incompatible with the aims and objectives of the United Nations and UNICEF, including sexual exploitation and abuse, sexual harassment, abuse of authority and discrimination based on gender, nationality, age, race, sexual orientation, religious or ethnic background or disabilities. UNICEF is committed to promote the protection and safeguarding of all children. All selected candidates will, therefore, undergo rigorous reference and background checks, and will be expected to adhere to these standards and principles. Background checks will include the verification of academic credential(s) and employment history. Selected candidates may be required to provide additional information to conduct a background check, and selected candidates with disabilities may be requested to submit supporting documentation in relation to their disability confidentially.

Qualified candidates are invited to submit the following documents via the online recruitment portal, TMS (Talent Management System):


An up-to-date TMS profile and curriculum vitae (CV)
Cover letter
A separate financial proposal using this template only TMC0002477 Financial proposal template.docx


Remarks: If the TOR or financial proposal documents are not visible on certain recruitment platforms, please visit our official page Vacancies | UNICEF Careers.

UNICEF does not charge a processing fee at any stage of its recruitment, selection, and hiring processes (i.e., application stage, interview stage, validation stage, or appointment and training). UNICEF will not ask for applicants’ bank account information.

All UNICEF positions are advertised, and only shortlisted candidates will be contacted and advance to the next stage of the selection process.

Additional information about working for UNICEF can be found here.

We are a world leading Engine MRO service provider, headquartered in Zurich, Switzerland, with over 90 years of operational experience. Our unwavering dedication to innovation, excellence and environmental responsibility propels us forward on our journey to becoming the leading, most customer-centric, and sustainable Engine focused MRO worldwide.

Working with an extensive network of partners and business development offices in Europe, the USA, Asia and the Middle East, we offer comprehensive, fully customized solutions for the Maintenance, Repair and Overhaul of aircraft engines, airframes and components sales business including impeccable technical support to over 500 customers worldwide. Our mission: We safely power your flight. As an Intern Data Analyst (New Engines Department) you will involve working on digitalization improvement projects and supporting the Operations Team and Management to work more efficiently. The intern will support in creating Power Apps Tool and Power BI Dashboard enabling live data input, update and visualization dashboard.

Your tasks


Create Power Apps Tool supporting Engine Shop Visit Management
Create Power BI Dashboard supporting Operations Management Report
Review and optimize use of SharePoint with New Engines organization


Your Profile


Currently enrolled in an undergraduate or graduate level degree in IT, Application Development
Computer Skills: Proficient with Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, Outlook). Power Apps, Power BI
Fluent in English or German (B1 / B2)
Strong interest in the Aerospace MRO industry and to work in a regulated environment
Other Skills: Good interpersonal skills; analytical, problem solving, and decision-making skills, good presentation skills


We offer

We offer an attractive position in a global and dynamic company. Through competitive employment conditions, you will have the opportunity to develop both professionally and personally. Title: Data Science Specialist
Description:
At Nestlé Nutrition, our mission is to nurture a Healthier Generation. We aim to achieve this by leading the way in Research and Development of science proven nutrition solutions and by partnering with parents and healthcare professionals around the world. From pre-conception to adulthood, we are widening our reach and opening up new opportunities across the lifelong nutrition journey.

Position Snapshot

Location: Konolfingen, Switzerland

Company: Nestlé Product & Technology Center (NPTC) Nutrition

Act. Rate: Full-Time Act. Rate 100%

Type of contract: Permanent contract

What We Offer At Nestlé

Exciting opportunities to develop your career your way; across different brands, businesses, functions, and regions.

Flexible working arrangements - facilitating creativity and collaboration

A culture of respect, with diversity, equity and inclusion at its core. Learn more here.

A dynamic international environment empowering you to learn, develop and grow.

Don’t hesitate to connect with us during the recruitment process to learn more.

Position Summary

As a Data Science Specialist, you will lead predictive modeling initiatives and translate complex data into actionable insights to support product innovation within the Nutrition Innovation Unit. You will collaborate with cross-functional teams (Scientists, Sensory Specialist, Product Developers, and others) to ensure robust data connectivity, high analytical standards, and full compliance with company frameworks.

A Day in the Life of a Data Science Specialist


Lead the application of machine learning techniques (e.g., regression, classification, time-series forecasting) to solve complex business problems across domains such as product development and advanced analytics
Own the preparation of complex datasets, including data cleaning, transformation, and structuring, to enable robust modeling and high‑quality analysis.
Partner closely with domain experts and cross‑functional stakeholders to design, develop, and deliver impactful data science solutions aligned with business needs.
Deploy and operationalize models in production environments, ensuring performance, reliability, and scalability, using platforms such as Azure ML, Databricks, and Snowflake.
Monitor model performance and continuously optimize accuracy, robustness, and scalability over time.
Translate analytical insights into clear visualizations and structured narratives, effectively communicating recommendations to both technical and non‑technical audiences.


What Will Make You Successful


Advanced degree (MSc or higher) in Data Science, Statistics, Computer Science, or a related field
Extensive experience in data science within Life Sciences, Food, or Academia, applying agile development practices
Proven track record in building and deploying predictive models in production
Hands-on expertise with ML frameworks (TensorFlow, Keras, PyTorch, Scikit-learn) and statistical methods including Design of Experiments
Familiarity with APIs (REST, JSON) and data visualization tools such as Power BI, PowerApps, and Streamlit
Proficiency with cloud and data platforms including Azure, Databricks, Snowflake, and SQL databases.
Ability to explain complex technical concepts to non-technical audience
Strong collaboration skills, with experience working closely with cross-functional teams (scientists, sensory specialists, product developers)
Highly autonomous and self‑driven, with strong problem‑solving capabilities
Excellent communication skills in English


Nestlé is committed to the accessibility of its recruitment process. If you require an adaptation during the recruitment process, please do let us know so we can support you effectively. All information will be treated confidentially.

At Nestlé Research and Development in Konolfingen near Bern / Switzerland, we develop for Nestlé worldwide, new products, processes and packaging concepts for a broad range of products of our Nutrition, Dairy, Health Science and Coffee businesses. In addition, we also provide technical assistance to our factories.

#nestlecareerswitzerland

Title: Responsible Sourcing Data Analyst
Description:
YOUR MISSION

Located in Richemont offices in Bellevue/Plan-les-Ouates, she/he will report to the Responsible SourcingData&Tools Project Manager. The Responsible Sourcing analyst will be responsible for collecting, analyzing, and interpreting large data sets (ESG data, Suppliers data….) to extract valuable and actionable insights for Responsible Sourcing strategy, decision-making, objectives setting, reporting and disclosure. This role involves translating complex data into understandable information, identifying trends and providing recommendations that support strategic business decisions in line with Responsible Sourcing strategy.

As Responsible Sourcing Data Analyst, you will design reports and data visualizations and develop tools for internal - Responsible Sourcing experts, Group procurement - and external stakeholders - mainly Regions/Maisons procurement and sustainability function - including but not limited to the preparation of reports and materials to communicate responsible sourcing key metrics.

Ultimately, in a fast evolving Sustainability regulations context, this role will also contribute to the development of new sourcing key performance indicators, building a resilient Responsible Sourcing management system for the Group

The ideal candidate will possess strong analytical skills, a keen eye for detail, and the ability to communicate findings effectively to both technical and non-technical stakeholders

HOW WILL YOU MAKE AN IMPACT?

Data collection & Extraction


Lead efforts to integrate data from various sources into the department applications, leveraging automation tools to streamline data processes
Gather data from various sources, including databases, data warehouses, APIs, and external datasets.
Develop and maintain automated data collection processes where applicable.


Data Analysis & Modelling


Perform exploratory data analysis to identify patterns, trends, and anomalies.
Apply statistical methods and data modelling techniques to answer business questions.
Develop and execute queries to extract and manipulate data for analysis.


Key metrics, tools & application development


Design, development and maintenance of the department in-house applications, dashboards and key performance indicators
Monitor their respective performance, identify areas for optimization, and implement enhancements to improve usability, performance, and scalability
Document and record all developments
Present complex data findings in a clear, concise, and compelling manner to various audiences.


Data governance & Quality assurance


Support the Responsible Sourcing Data & Tools project manager to establish and enforce data governance policies and procedures
Clean, transform and validate data to ensure accuracy, completeness, and consistency of data used in Responsible Sourcing dashboards and reports.
Identify & Resolve data quality issues.


Support, Collaboration and Communication


Collaborate closely with Responsible Sourcing experts to understand business requirements, gather insights, and update dashboards, reports
Provide technical support and training to end-users of Responsible sourcing dashboards, tools and applications


Others Transversal Related Tasks


Support the Responsible Sourcing team in the development of supply chains ESG key metrics to align with sustainability frameworks and regulations – e.g GRI standards, CSRD, CSDDDD…


HOW WILL YOU EXPERIENCE SUCCESS WITH US?


Education: Bachelor's degree in a quantitative field such as Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Economics, Business Analytics, or a related discipline.
Experience:
2-5 years of experience in a data analysis role.
Proven experience with data manipulation, analysis, and reporting.
Technical Skills:
SQL Proficiency: Strong ability to write complex SQL queries for data extraction, manipulation, and analysis.
Data Visualization Tools: Hands-on experience with at least Looker
Spreadsheet Software: Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel (pivot tables, VLOOKUP, advanced formulas, data modelling).
Programming (Optional but highly valued): Familiarity with Python or R for data analysis, statistical modelling, and automation.
Analytical & Problem-Solving Skills:
Strong analytical and critical thinking abilities to interpret data and draw meaningful conclusions.
Ability to identify root causes of problems and propose data-driven solutions.
Excellent attention to detail and accuracy.
Communication Skills:
Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to present complex information clearly and concisely.
Ability to effectively communicate with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Business Acumen:
Understanding of business processes and the ability to connect data insights to business objectives.
Curiosity and a proactive approach to understanding business challenges.
Teamwork & Collaboration:
Ability to work independently and as part of a cross-functional team.
Strong interpersonal skills.

Preferred Skills


Master's degree in a quantitative field.
Experience with cloud data platforms (e.g., Google Cloud Platform).
Knowledge of data warehousing concepts and ETL processes.
Understanding of machine learning concepts and their application in business.

Title: Online Data Analyst
Description:
Online Data Analyst

Are you detail-oriented with a passion for research and a good grasp of national and local geography? This freelance role lets you work at your own pace from home!

Role Overview:

Put your research skills to work improving the digital maps that millions of people rely on every day.

You'll conduct web-based evaluations to verify data accuracy, compare information across sources, and ensure the mapping content powering global navigation services is precise and relevant—all while working from home on your own schedule!

Requirements:

Fluency in English and German
Residency in Switzerland for the past two years
Familiarity with local business, media, sports, news, social media, and culture in Switzerland
Ability to follow guidelines and conduct online research
Flexibility to handle diverse tasks including maps, news, and audio
Daily access to broadband internet, computer, and relevant software
Willingness to work on a freelance basis

Recruitment Process:

To join the program, after you complete your application you will be invited to take an open book qualification exam. We will provide guidelines and learning materials in advance, and you can complete the exam at your convenience within a specific timeframe.

As part of our hiring process, candidates who progress past the initial screening and qualification stages may be asked to complete a third-party identity and background verification. This verification is conducted through a secure external provider and may require you to provide government-issued identification and other documentation at that later stage.

Please note we do not use this information to make initial application screening decisions.

Why join the TELUS Digital AI Community?

Flexibility: Work at your own pace from home
Global impact: Be part of a 1million+ strong AI Community
Innovation: Support machine learning models for world-leading brands
Growth: No previous professional experience required
Long-term opportunity: This is a part-time, long-term, independent freelance position

Equal Opportunity:

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for a contractual relationship without regards to race, colour, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or protected veteran status. At Telus Digital AI, we are proud to offer equal opportunities and are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive community. All aspects of selection are based on applicants’ qualifications, merits, competence, and performance without regards to any characteristic related to diversity.

Apply today to join our dynamic and innovative team!

Title: Supply Chain Data & Analytics, Senior Analyst
Description:
VF Corporation is looking for an exceptional Supply Chain Data & Analytics, Senior Analyst to join our team based in Stabio, Switzerland.

Let’s talk about the role!

As Supply Chain Data & Analytics, Senior Analyst, you will identify and provide insights focusing on various Supply Chain areas throughout the go-to-market process. You will also assist your manager in reporting and analysing strategic Supply Chain KPIs to provide regular updates to the Supply Chain Community, Supply Chain Regional & Global Leadership, and to Brand representatives. Lastly, you will be active part of strategic transformation initiatives aimed to create new reporting/analytics solutions supporting key stakeholders in taking data-driven and more informed business decisions.

Get ready to make an impact! Here’s what you’ll be responsible for, joining our team as Supply Chain Data & Analytics, Senior Analyst:

Day-by-Day Reporting


support the preparation of weekly/monthly reporting to be distributed to Supply Chain Community and to the Brands (i.e. On-Time Delivery to Customers, On-Time Stock Availability, Inventory Summary, DC Processing Performance, …)
support Supply Chain Operations teams with ad-hoc analysis and quick insights when required


Business Analytics


explore data and analyze processes to identify opportunity for optimization / improvement of our business metrics
leveraging on Supply Chain processes knowledge and analytics skills to formulate hypothesis, interpret and properly communicate results to the stakeholders


Analytics Tools and Solutions


collaborate with Supply Chain functions and other business stakeholders to identify and formalize reporting/analytics requirements, and translate them into Analytics Solutions
collaborate with Digital & Technology Analytics and/or Business Intelligence functions to validate and bring live new Analytics models/solutions
support the Supply Chain Data & Analytics Lead / Manager in the preparation and review of Business Requirement Documents related to new Analytics/Reporting requests; connect then with Digital & Technology teams to make sure the technical solution is aligned with business requirements provided
as member of Supply Chain business function, provide feedback and validate the reporting solutions and KPIs developed by Technology team
ensure the accuracy, transparency and explainability of all data shared with Supply Chain functions and with the brands
socialize new analytics solutions created/deployed, train business stakeholders and promote the adoption


Skills For Success

We all have unique skills that we bring to work and celebrate every day. The foundation skills you will need in this position are:


basic knowledge of Supply Chain processes across the full go-to-market journey (Inbound Logistics, DC Operations, Outbound Logistics, …)
fluent in English with excellent verbal and written communication skills
passionate for insight-driven thinking, can extract patterns from volumes of data to drive insights
strong attention to details, and commitment to drive data accuracy
advanced knowledge of MS Suire tools
advanced knowledge of MS Power BI as visualization tool
IBM Cognos experience is a plus
predisposition to apply AI tools within the day-by-day tasks and openness to explore AI-based approach for advanced Analytics solutions
ability to work independently, on multiple tasks, and prioritize effectively in a high-paced environment
strong judgment and decision-making capabilities and ability to identify problems and propose creative, effective, and practical solutions
excellent story-telling capabilities, ability to summarize big volumes of data results in clear business language insights and actions
team player with a positive attitude, high degree of personal integrity, and ability to foster trust, respect, and collaboration with colleagues


About VF

VF is a global apparel company connecting people to the lifestyles they cherish through our family of iconic brands. At the heart of our journey lies our purpose: We power movements of sustainable and active lifestyles for the betterment of people and our planet. This is our purpose. It’s the reason we come to work every day. Our purpose unites us and leads us to pursue our goals, together. This is our calling. Learn more at vfc.com

What’s In It For You

We’re in the business of unleashing human potential, driven by the ideas, energy and commitment of our people. That’s why we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical and financial well-being for all VF associates. When it comes to benefits, here is the total package:


a supportive feedback-based culture where respect and integrity guide us in what we do
an inclusive international environment where people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles and nationalities love working together
be part of an iconic lifestyle brand in a multi brand, multi countries organization
on-site gym offering health and well-being initiatives
a discount card with 50% on all VF brands
break-out areas offering complimentary hot drinks


Free to Be, Inclusion & Diversity

As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day.

If you like what you have read and want to join our team, then we would like to hear from you!

Title: Data Engineer, Product
Description:
This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Data Engineer, Product based in Switzerland.

This role sits at the core of a high-impact data and machine learning ecosystem, powering the systems that drive user engagement, personalization, and product growth at scale. You will be responsible for building and maintaining robust data pipelines that transform large-scale datasets into reliable inputs for ML models and recommendation systems. The environment is highly collaborative, working closely with ML Engineers and product teams to shape data foundations that directly influence user experience and business performance. You will take full ownership of end-to-end data workflows, ensuring scalability, reliability, and performance across complex data systems. The team operates in a lean, high-ownership setup where engineers are expected to drive solutions from design through to production. This is a hands-on role with strong exposure to machine learning-driven product development in a fast-scaling digital marketplace.

Accountabilities


Design, build, and maintain scalable ETL/ELT data pipelines that transform raw data into high-quality datasets for machine learning and product use cases.
Develop and optimize data transformation workflows supporting feature engineering for both offline model training and online inference systems.
Collaborate closely with ML Engineers to understand data requirements and deliver reliable inputs for recommendation systems and predictive models.
Ensure strong data quality, governance, and monitoring across all pipelines to guarantee accuracy, reliability, and consistency of datasets.
Own data pipelines end-to-end, including design, implementation, deployment, monitoring, and continuous improvement.
Improve performance, scalability, and efficiency of large-scale data processing systems, including batch and near real-time workloads.
Contribute to building robust data foundations that enable experimentation, personalization, and ML-driven product innovation.


Requirements


5+ years of experience in Data Engineering or a similar role within data-intensive or product-driven environments.
Strong hands-on experience with Apache Spark and Python for large-scale data processing and transformation.
Solid knowledge of SQL and experience designing and working with data models and transformation logic.
Proven experience building and maintaining ETL/ELT pipelines with end-to-end ownership.
Experience working with high-volume data systems, including batch and/or near real-time processing pipelines.
Strong ability to collaborate with Machine Learning and product teams in ML-driven environments.
Familiarity with Databricks is a plus.
Experience with streaming technologies (e.g., Kafka, Flink), feature stores, or ML data workflows is highly desirable.
Strong problem-solving mindset with attention to scalability, performance, and data reliability.


Benefits


Competitive salary aligned with senior data engineering market standards (€64,800-€74,400 annually referenced in original posting)
Employee stock option program
Performance-based bonuses and referral rewards
Flexible remote-first working model with location autonomy
Personal learning and professional development budget
Additional paid leave options
Paid volunteering opportunities
Opportunity to work remotely while traveling
High-growth environment focused on product innovation and ML-driven systems


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Internship Global Master Data Analyst 80-100% (m/f/d)
Description:
Switzerland, Visp

Today, Lonza is a global leader in life sciences operating across five continents. While we work in science, there’s no magic formula to how we do it. Our greatest scientific solution is talented people working together, devising ideas that help businesses to help people. In exchange, we let our people own their careers. Their ideas, big and small, genuinely improve the world. And that’s the kind of work we want to be part of.

What You Will Do

The Master Data department in Visp is a global team responsible for the development of master data processes and standards that are to be followed by the whole organization. As an intern, you will play an active role in:


Perform various data analyses and reports to support Master data or Business projects
Assisting with SAP Master Data mass maintenance and migration efforts
Gathering feedback from global and local Subject Matter Experts (SMEs) across the organization
Defining functional and technical requirements for the development or enhancement of Master Data processes
Updating and improving training materials, and delivering hands-on training sessions to teams across Lonza


As a part of the Global Master Data team, you will be able to learn SAP Materials Management (MM), Production & Planning (PP) and Sales & Distribution (SD) modules, key business processes as well as SAP data structures. Overall, you will interact with many different departments and experts across the company worldwide.

What We Are Looking For


A Bachelor’s or Master’s degree (or equivalent experience) in data science, computer science, business information technology, or mathematics
Solid understanding of data structures or hands-on experience with databases
Proficiency in English; German and other languages are a valuable plus
A passion for optimizing processes, improving IT tools, and exploring innovative solutions
You are interested in being a reliable team member in a multi-cultural environment.


About Lonza

At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.

Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. Title: Audit Technology and Data Analytics Expert, Director Analyst
Description:
This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, which manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for an Audit Technology and Data Analytics Expert, Director Analyst based in Switzerland.

This is a senior thought leadership role focused on shaping how global organizations design and execute data-driven audit and risk strategies. You will produce high-impact research, insights, and frameworks that guide senior audit and risk leaders in leveraging advanced analytics and emerging technologies. The role combines deep analytical expertise with strong communication and advisory skills, enabling you to influence executive decision-making across large enterprises, government bodies, and global institutions. You will engage directly with clients through briefings, presentations, and advisory conversations, translating complex data and technology trends into actionable strategies. Working within a highly collaborative research environment, you will help define best practices and future directions for audit analytics and digital risk capabilities. This is a highly visible role for an expert who thrives on intellectual rigor, thought leadership, and global influence.

Accountabilities


Develop high-impact research and thought leadership on audit data analytics, creating actionable insights, frameworks, and best practices for senior risk and audit leaders.
Analyze market trends, emerging technologies, and industry shifts to produce forward-looking guidance for global organizations.
Conduct deep analytical work to identify root causes of client challenges and translate findings into strategic recommendations.
Deliver presentations, briefings, and advisory sessions to senior stakeholders in both virtual and in-person settings.
Contribute to content development, peer review, and refinement of research outputs within a global analyst community.
Support sales and client engagement efforts by providing subject matter expertise and market insights that strengthen client relationships.
Mentor junior analysts and contribute to improving research methodologies, processes, and delivery standards.
Represent the organization’s research perspective at conferences, industry events, and executive forums.


Requirements


10+ years of relevant professional experience, including strong exposure to internal audit, data analytics, or risk functions.
At least 3 years of direct experience in data analytics within internal audit, and 7+ years in audit or related risk/data roles.
Proven thought leadership in audit analytics strategy, including development of frameworks, governance models, and advanced analytics capabilities.
Strong understanding of risk domains such as cybersecurity, cloud security, governance, resilience, third-party risk, and data protection.
Exceptional analytical skills with the ability to synthesize complex data, identify patterns, and deliver structured insights.
Strong research and writing capabilities, with experience producing executive-level content and strategic recommendations.
Excellent communication and presentation skills, with confidence engaging senior stakeholders and large audiences.
Strong business and financial acumen with an understanding of global market dynamics in audit and risk.
Ability to work independently while collaborating effectively in global, multicultural, matrixed environments.
Experience speaking at conferences or industry events is highly desirable.
Willingness to travel up to 25% as required.


Benefits


Competitive compensation package with strong performance-based rewards.
Hybrid and flexible working model supporting both remote and in-person collaboration.
Opportunity to influence global audit and risk practices at executive and industry level.
Access to world-class research resources, tools, and global expert networks.
Strong professional development opportunities within a leading global insights organization.
Inclusive, diverse, and highly collaborative international work environment.
Opportunity to contribute to cutting-edge topics including AI, data analytics, and digital transformation in risk and audit.

Title: Online Data Analyst
Description:
Online Data Analyst

Are you detail-oriented with a passion for research and a good grasp of national and local geography? This freelance role lets you work at your own pace from home!

Role Overview:

Put your research skills to work improving the digital maps that millions of people rely on every day.

You'll conduct web-based evaluations to verify data accuracy, compare information across sources, and ensure the mapping content powering global navigation services is precise and relevant—all while working from home on your own schedule!

Requirements:

Fluency in English and German
Residency in Switzerland for the past two years
Familiarity with local business, media, sports, news, social media, and culture in Switzerland
Ability to follow guidelines and conduct online research
Flexibility to handle diverse tasks including maps, news, and audio
Daily access to broadband internet, computer, and relevant software
Willingness to work on a freelance basis

Recruitment Process:

To join the program, after you complete your application you will be invited to take an open book qualification exam. We will provide guidelines and learning materials in advance, and you can complete the exam at your convenience within a specific timeframe.

As part of our hiring process, candidates who progress past the initial screening and qualification stages may be asked to complete a third-party identity and background verification. This verification is conducted through a secure external provider and may require you to provide government-issued identification and other documentation at that later stage.

Please note we do not use this information to make initial application screening decisions.

Why join the TELUS Digital AI Community?

Flexibility: Work at your own pace from home
Global impact: Be part of a 1million+ strong AI Community
Innovation: Support machine learning models for world-leading brands
Growth: No previous professional experience required
Long-term opportunity: This is a part-time, long-term, independent freelance position

Equal Opportunity:

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for a contractual relationship without regards to race, colour, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or protected veteran status. At Telus Digital AI, we are proud to offer equal opportunities and are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive community. All aspects of selection are based on applicants’ qualifications, merits, competence, and performance without regards to any characteristic related to diversity.

Apply today to join our dynamic and innovative team!

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
About the Role

Imagine this you step into a role where data is not just infrastructure it is the backbone of every critical decision and breakthrough. As a Data Engineer you will take ownership of evolving and strengthening our data ecosystem transforming fragmented pipelines into scalable high-performance systems. This is a hands-on role where you directly remove bottlenecks unlock faster processing and enable teams across the company to access reliable high-quality data. Your work will set the foundation for future data visualization capabilities and smarter metadata management powering the next wave of innovation in our aerospace systems.

At Destinus, we are shaping the future of defense through high-speed, autonomous flight technologies. We develop next-generation unmanned aerial systems that deliver greater speed, precision, and efficiency, supporting governments and defense organizations in mission-critical operations worldwide.

Daedalean AG, the AI and autonomy hub of the Destinus Group, plays a key role in this vision. Developing safety-critical AI systems at the intersection of advanced research and real-world aviation. Enabling machines to perceive, understand, and make decisions in real time.

Together, we combine aerospace engineering and artificial intelligence to build the next generation of autonomous flight systems, driving innovation where it matters most.

What You'll Do

· Restructure and maintain existing data pipelines to increase operational flexibility, ensuring non-standard data formats and incoming project requests can be efficiently processed.

· Provide dedicated support and maintenance for current data tooling to eliminate workflow bottlenecks.

· Lead the next phase of data infrastructure by designing and building advanced tools for granular data visualization and manipulation.

· Implement and manage robust metadata frameworks to provide a comprehensive understanding of existing data assets.

· Monitor, troubleshoot, and resolve issues within the data architecture to ensure reliability and performance.



Requirements




What You'll Need

Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Data Engineering, or a related

· quantitative field or equivalent practical experience.

· Proven track record in data engineering, with specific experience in building, maintaining, and restructuring complex data pipelines (ETL/ELT).

· Strong programming skills and proficiency in SQL are a must, C++, Go and Rust will be a plus.

· Hands-on experience with cloud object storage, specifically Amazon S3, and version control systems (git) for managing codebase changes.

· Familiarity with modern data processing frameworks and database management systems.

· Familiarity with metadata management practices and data visualization concepts.

· Strong analytical skills with a proven ability to identify bottlenecks and optimize systems for speed, quality, and scale.

Who You Are

You thrive when you have ownership and autonomy. You do not wait for tasks to come to you; you take initiative and keep things moving. You are detail-driven but also practical, understanding that speed and accuracy need to coexist. You enjoy being in the middle of operations, where your work has immediate impact. When things get messy, you bring structure. When processes break, you fix them. You are reliable, proactive, and ready to take control of your domain.
